programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on plant-based food, cooking a 'buddha bowl' with onion bhajis, with vegetable grower Bryde Marshall and actress Jane McCarry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures, which include West Ham United v Aston Villa and Leicester City v Manchester United

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Gabby Logan look back at some of the most memorable moments by British athletes at Olympic Games, counting down the top 25 moments from Britain's Summer Olympics. Some of the highlights expected to feature are the battle between Seb Coe and Steve Cram in the 1500m at Los Angeles 1984, where Daley Thompson also made headlines in the Decathlon. Steve Redgrave's attempt to claim a fifth rowing gold medal at Sydney 2000 is sure to be high up the list, as are Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell's exploits in the men's 100m and women's 400m hurdles at Barcelona 1992. London 2012 saw Team GB claim a record number of gold medals, with sporting luminaries such as Bradley Wiggins, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Andy Murray and Mo Farah proving why they were household names, and new stars such as Nicola Adams, Laura Trott and Anthony Joshua burst onto the sporting landscape

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Comedian Paul Chuckle, actress Amanda Henderson, comic and writer Geoff Norcott and chef and entrepreneur Levi Roots tackle questions on English football clubs, songs from animated Disney films, the Star Wars prequel trilogy and reggae music

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Olympics edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Anthony Ogogo, Helen Glover, Sir Matthew Pinsent, James Cracknell, Nick Skelton, JJ Chalmers, Jeanette Kwakye and Mark Foster

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: With Lev acting distant, Faith struggles to cope whilst waiting for the results of Luka's latest scan. In a crisis, she calls Dylan, asking to see a friendly face, and he agrees to be there to support her. Meanwhile, Ethan bumps into Fenisha at Holby Comic Con and the pair are delighted to see each other. However, things soon turn awry when a van is accidently driven into a merchandise stall, seriously injuring several people, and Ethan is suddenly struck by terrifying memories of last year's terrorist attack

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Iron Man 3 (2013)
programme.desc: The armoured avenger hunts for the mastermind behind an untraceable series of bombings, despite being cut off from his usual resources and traumatised by a recent brush with death. His search uncovers a secret conspiracy involving a drug that renders the user indestructible. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Gwyneth Paltrow and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Glaswegian geek Milan snoops around the bedrooms of potential partners, hoping to meet a woman who loves the great outdoors and shares his love of Star Wars and superheroes

programme.title: In the Dark Half (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager babysits a six-year-old neighbour, but is distraught when he unexpectedly dies in her care. While the dead child's father looks for someone to blame, she is tormented by the belief that his spirit is haunting the abandoned bunker she has made her refuge from her troubles. Horror, starring Jessica Barden, Tony Curran and Lyndsey Marshal

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sally Phillips and Angellica Bell take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of office space in Tring, Hertfordshire, a house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Lancashire and a semi-detached in Ambergate, Derby, after they are sold at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is on Crosby Beach celebrating the 15th anniversary of Antony Gormley's Another Place iron figure sculptures, takling to local Christians inspired by them to create their own artworks

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Amar Latif presents an appeal on behalf of Retina UK, a charity that provides support and information for those with inherited sight loss, and aslo funds research into potential treatments

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is with a Norfolk father and daughter, who hope their £700k budget will afford them an equestrian property and annexe to house their family and horse

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley searching for three skip-bound items. Bruce Faulseit repurposes copper pipes, while Daniel Heath comes up with a radical redesign for two old tables

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock travel to Swindon, competing to transform an uninspiring garden into a welcoming and warm outdoor space, perfect for entertaining or dining

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Chester, where the buyer wants something to mark her 10th year of singing in an award-winning choir. With £500 on offer, will it be a glass maker who works on a barge, a collage artist who re-works magazines, or a textile worker of wool who makes the buyer's day? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad introduces reports and reaction from the final round of Premier League fixtures, as the 2019/20 top-flight season reaches its climax. With analysis from Garth Crooks and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani visit Chichester Harbour, the only Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in the UK that is managed by a harbour authority, discovering how the needs of a very busy harbour are balanced with those of nature and wildlife. While Matt is out on the water doing the daily rounds with the harbour master, Anita finds out about projects helping to protect local populations of oysters, terns and seals. Plus, Adam Henson's Highland bull Archie might not have long left the farm, but his legacy lives on

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire. Items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems, a Cartier clock, a tiny bowl with a giant price tag, a hat once given to Ronald Reagan and a walking stick belonging to Scottish music hall star Harry Lauder

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: In a newly independent India in 1951, passionate literature student Lata Mehra is looking towards her own future as she sets out to make her way in the world. She is not interested in romance, but her resolve is challenged when she starts to fall for a mysterious fellow student Kabir Durrani. Meanwhile, religious tensions mount between Muslims and Hindus in the wake of Partition, as India's first national democratic election approaches. Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the final round of Premier League fixtures, with the curtain coming down on the 2019-20 campaign almost 12 months after it began. European qualification and the relegation battle could both go down to the wire, with fixtures including Leicester City v Manchester United, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Ham United v Aston Villa. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles, Takumi, the Colonel and Alaska reunite for revenge after the Weekday Warriors go too far, while secrets are revealed and romance blossoms during a game of Best Day/Worst Day

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles and Lara are officially dating, and Alaska is not thrilled, while Sara warns the Colonel about the dire consequences of the prank they pulled on the Weekday Warriors

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the sales of a terrace house in Hornchurch, a piece of land in Newquay, and a commercial space in Rochdale

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A veteran racing driver is involved in a horrific crash on the finish line, two friends fish a hand grenade out of a river and a young boy alerts his mum to a car fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets an Action Man collector to learn more about this much-loved toy

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A revelation spreads through the residents, upsetting many and injuring relationships. Australian drama following life in an inner-city neighbourhood

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a home in the north Cornwall countryside with a budget of £525,000, and also visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion set out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield, but a messy encounter with a gull almost ruins one deal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Gillian Taylforth and Adam Woodyatt, aka mother and son Kathy and Ian Beale, who tell all about their unique working relationship and 35-year friendship. They talk about their biggest scenes - from Gillian's nerves while filming Kathy's shock return from the dead to the real reason Adam had to wear a hat throughout Ian's homeless storyline

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: A wildly successful music producer who is one of Speedi-Kazz's most loyal customers offers Kazim and Jeremiah the chance to use his studio for free - and they jump at the chance to record a promotional song for their delivery service

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Evan Biddell hosts as the contestants take on an eco-inspired design challenge, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Brother and sister Harry and Lilly conjure up a spellbinding extravaganza for their two mothers with a Witches and Wizards-themed wedding. Presenter Ed Petrie is joined by Harry Potter star Afshan Azad and Miss Drill from The Worst Witch to help the duo create a day of flying, fireballs and chocolate magic potions

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Odd animal transportation tales, including the poisonous frog helping to make air travel more safe and a convoy of caterpillars on the lookout for a new home

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in an onion-eating competition in Newent

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Community on Solid Ground is a youth centre in Whalley Range. Before its current management took over the building the area didn't offer much for the youngsters living there and anti-social behaviour was very common. That has dropped by 73 per cent since Community on Solid Ground came along, the kids can hang out, play sports and have a place to go that means they aren't hanging around the streets. Sadly the facilities are very tired and they don't have areas they can play their beloved sport on, so the Dengineers are going to transform their outside space into a sports arena worthy of the Olympics. Presented by Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Ecuador in search of ocean giants and the biggest baby animal on earth.

programme.title: Britain's Most Sensational Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Piers Taylor challenge three backyard inventors to let their imaginations run wild and come up with unique and visually stunning designs, including a sweet-dispensing carousel, an automated breakfast maker and a giant robot

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. Conservationist Giles Clark continues his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. At the sanctuary, Mary the sun bear is now nine months old. Now growing in confidence, she is becoming extremely determined, and picks up a skill unique to a sun bear, jumping. Later, the sanctuary receives some shocking news when they discover there has been a break-in, and the distraught team have to come to terms with a theft

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: A journey along America's West coast. The programme examines how the landscape is shaped by the Pacific Ocean as well as powerful forces beneath ground, particularly along the San Andreas Fault. The programme also visits the warm and sheltered waters of the Sea of Cortez, where animals including blue whales gather, and discovers how a small community in America's westernmost point have a unique relationship with grey whales

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson presents a guide to Italian cookery, demonstrating how to create traditional and exciting meals using ingredients that are widely available in most supermarkets. She begins by preparing Sicilian pasta with tomatoes, garlic and almonds, a family feast of tagliata and Tuscan fries, and a fiery egg dish designed for late-night parties

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues a tired rocking chair, old scaffolding poles and a pair of dining chairs from the tip, enlisting Kev Paxton and Simion Hawton-Smith to help transform them

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is tasked with helping a couple and their young sons to quit London and make a new life and home in Derbyshire, and spends time with an artisan knife-maker

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall celebrates volunteers who help wildlife in the West Country, meeting a woman who has transformed her home into a hedgehog hospital

programme.title: Animals with Cameras
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan and a team of scientists learn more about various species through the use of animal bodycams. In Namibia, they help conservationist Marlice van Vuuren find out whether the three orphaned cheetahs she has raised have developed the skills needed to hunt for themselves in the wild, with close-up footage revealing how the cats choose their prey. On Kanowna Island off the south coast of Australia, Gordon discovers how the fur seal population manages to thrive and hunt in the surrounding shark-infested waters, and in South Africa, cameras gives an insight into the lives of wild baboons

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade along the way. This time, Giles and Monica help out at The Torridon in the Scottish Highlands, Britain's most northerly five-star hotel. Its need to balance a luxury stay with being self-sufficient has Monica collecting seaweed for the kitchen and helping to herd Highland cattle, while Giles learns to change toilet seats and joins the staff in their afternoon off - for a swim in the beautiful but freezing-cold loch

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Music festival goers descend on Glasgow, a street art mystery is revealed, and the oldest and youngest train cleaners on the station set to work

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga charts the history of the Liverpool house from 1945 to the present day. Young newlyweds moved into the attic at the end of the war, but then the house fell into rapid decline and after a family of seven stayed there, it was marked for demolition, narrowly avoiding the wrecking ball by a strange twist of fate. Abandoned, it was eventually rescued in the 1970s by an enterprising group of local activists and turned into a home for creative people. Finally, David meets the current owner Gaynor, who has restored the home to its former glory

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from day two of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. The Windies may still be a long way from reproducing the quality of their incredible teams of the 1970s and 80s, but their new pace attack has been heralded as one of the strongest in world cricket, and should provide a great test for the English batsmen, whose time at the crease of late has understandably been limited

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy explores how American history has been mythologised and manipulated by generations of politicians, writers and protesters. She begins by examining the American Revolution - a David-and-Goliath battle of men with high ideals taking on the might of the British Empire. But how much of America's founding story is based on fact?

programme.title: World Cup 1966: Alfie's Boys
programme.desc: David Jason presents a documentary about how Alf Ramsey recruited the England football team that won the world cup in 1966, and was able to unite the members into a world class side. Featuring archive footage of the team in action and contributions from Bobby Charlton, Jack Charlton, Jimmy Greaves, George Cohen, Tina Moore, Harry Redknapp, Terry Venables, Geoff Hurst and Gordon Banks

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: A special episode of the programme dedicated to Jack Charlton, the World Cup winner and former Republic of Ireland manager, who died earlier this month at the age of 85

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: The first of three special editions of the archive music show focusing on hits from the 1960s, including songs by the Foundations, Julie Driscoll, Sandie Shaw and Procol Harum. Narrated by Mark Radcliffe

programme.title: Just Jim (2015)
programme.desc: A Welsh teenager leads a lonely life after being rejected by his only friend. He becomes fascinated by a mysterious American who moves in next door, and the new arrival offers him some advice on how to be cool. The youngster starts to develop the life he had always longed for, but begins to wonder if he was happier the way he used to be. Comedy drama, starring Emile Hirsch and Craig Roberts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey of dishes beginning with the letter T, with Rachel Khoo cooking trout in a parcel and Raymond Blanc preparing the traditional dessert tarte tatin

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke head to Croydon to create exotic, Jamaican-style garden for owner Carmen, who needs a tranquil space to remember her lost daughter

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap are in the Shropshire Hills to find out about an ambitious project to create a nature corridor between the two peaks of Long Mynd and Stiperstones. Tom meets a farmer who's become a convert to environmentally friendly regenerative farming, while Ellie goes in search of rare and much misunderstood wildlife. Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals the progress of his tomatoes and gives advice on the summer maintenance of ponds. Nick Bailey travels to South Africa to search for the wild cousins of vibrant summer flower the crocosmia, while garden historian Advolly Richmond heads to Alton Towers in Staffordshire to take a close look at the Victorian trend of creating theme parks in their gardens. Plus, a second chance to meet a couple in Staffordshire who are very particular about the colours of the plants they use in the borders of their cottage garden

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Kirsty Wilson go all exotic with bamboos and aeoniums. In contrast, Sophie McKilligan gives an update from her Aberdeen allotment, while George Anderson discusses the art of getting watering right

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef heads for the region of Extremadura, famous for paprika and Iberico ham. He then concludes his exploration of the country by driving south in his camper van to the city of Seville - the capital of tapas and flamenco

programme.title: The Good Cook
programme.desc: Food writer Simon Hopkinson demonstrates how to create restaurant-quality food at home, preparing cheese and onion pie, Rockefeller-style mussels, Tuscan tomato and bread salad, tender breast of lamb with onions and orange caramel custard. He also discusses his love of sourdough bread

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley tries her hand at painting in an exploration of self-expression, while Ashley John-Baptiste discovers how music is being used to give people new opportunities

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary charting life along the Zambezi River, set against the spectacular backdrop of the Victoria Falls, and told through the experiences of local angler Mr White, who has fished the waters for 69 years. Over the decades, his companions have been the area's wildlife - elephants, baboons, hippos and kingfishers - and the film shows how their lives are ruled by the seasonal changes of the river and the rains

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist continues to explore the secrets behind evolution, this time focusing on the animal kingdom's bizarre hybrids, including the 'pizzly bear', a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which is thought to have come into being as a result of global warming. He also covers the killer bee brought into existence due to the transfer of African bees to South America

programme.title: Deep Impact (1998)
programme.desc: A TV reporter investigating a presidential misdemeanour stumbles on evidence that a comet is on a collision course with Earth, threatening the future of the human race. Meanwhile, an astronaut prepares to lead his crew on a do-or-die mission to intercept the danger. Sci-fi drama, starring Robert Duvall, Tea Leoni, Morgan Freeman and Elijah Wood

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part series in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their country's current crises

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from day three of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. The tourists picked up an impressive victory in the first game of the series earlier this month, beating England by four wickets at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. Chasing 200 on the final day, the Windies were reduced to 27-3 and lost opener John Campbell retired hurt, but Jermaine Blackwood calmly combined with Roston Chase for a stand of 73, then added 68 with Shane Dowrich, to see them home

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The panel oversee more business propositions, with returning Dragon Theo Paphitis joining regulars Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two entrepreneurs seeking funding for a range of drinks go to the lengths of setting up a bar in the studio, while other items up for assessment include a design for a pipe-fitting tool and a range of men's cosmetics. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: Romesh concludes the series in which he revisits the nine countries he travelled to on the series from the safety of his sofa, looking back on adventures that took him a long way off the regular tourist trail. In this final episode, he catches up with old friends he met on his journeys around Colombia, Haiti and Ethiopia, and showcases both some of the most memorable moments from those episodes and some previously unseen footage - including a chance to see his disastrous attempt to drive a tuk-tuk

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis explores the American attitude to death as he visits Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets people battling against serious illnesses. He talks to patients and their families as they face whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatments to prolong their lives, and meets the doctors whose job it is to guide people through the traumatic process

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Sitcom pilot broadcast in real time, starring Lee Mack as a man enduring the worst 20 minutes of his life. When his girlfriend goes into labour, Stuart is forced to rely on his ex-wife to help get her to hospital - but he also has to contend with his promiscuous father, estranged brother, and a naked neighbour who has had an accident with a circular saw

programme.title: Frankie Boyle Live: Excited for You to See and Hate This
programme.desc: The comedian takes to the stage of the King's Theatre in his home town of Glasgow after completing his first major tour of Scotland in 12 years. Frankie turns his attention to a number of diverse subjects, including Glasgow's relationship with swearing and vitamins, British politics, his concerns about ageing, buying a dog and the nature of comedy itself

programme.title: The Salesman (2016)
programme.desc: A woman is attacked in her apartment and her husband is determined to find the assailant, despite her objections. Iranian drama, starring Shahab Hosseini and Taraneh Alidoosti. In Persian and English

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: Documentary examining the realities of war and life under Isis, with unique personal archive from civilians and soldiers from both sides of the conflict. As war began, many Iraqis were hopeful. Ahmed Al Bashir, now a renowned comedian, remembers practising his English with US troops before the realities of occupation became clear. Waleed Nesyif was 18 when George Bush gave Saddam Hussein just 48 hours to leave Iraq. He was, like many Iraqi teenagers at that time, infatuated by the West. But while many of his generation grew up enjoying songs by the Backstreet Boys, Waleed formed Iraq's first heavy metal band. Narrated by Andy Serkis

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian returns to Darwin Ward, determined to make amends with Jac. But when forgiveness does not come easy, Kian goes into self-destruct mode - with almost fatal consequences. Meanwhile, a broken Essie searches for her purpose, but where will she find the answer she seeks? Ric suspects Guy has fallen off the wagon and sets out to stop him working at Holby, once and for all

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes a look at claims that diet drinks could be making people gain weight, Gloria Hunniford finds out whether all carbs should be avoided or if some are the key to a healthy lifestyle and Danny Crates looks at whether greasy and sugary foods affect people's skin

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show

programme.title: Lost Land of the Tiger
programme.desc: Alan Rabinowitz, Gordon Buchanan, George McGavin, Steve Backshall and Justine Evans travel to the far-flung corners of the Himalayas in search of tigers, snow leopards and clouded leopards, on an expedition aimed at helping the survival of these creatures. The team gets to work by tracking elusive tigers and setting up remote-controlled cameras to film the animals in action

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The dung beetle, an insect revered by ancient Egyptians, uses the sun, the moon and even the Milky Way to move its prized ball of dung in the right direction. Pigeons are often considered feeble birdbrains, but they have incredible memories that can recall several complex travel routes with amazing accuracy and they even use man-made roads and hedgerows to find the quickest way home. Here, the veteran broadcaster profiles animals with extraordinary abilities for finding the way

programme.title: Laura (1944)
programme.desc: A woman presumed murdered turns up alive and well, confounding the suspicions of the New York detective working on the case, who thought the killer could have been one of her many socialite friends. Meanwhile, he finds himself irresistibly drawn to her as he tries to establish the victim's real identity. Mystery, with Gene Tierney, Dana Andrews, Clifton Webb and Vincent Price

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian go head to head at a car boot sale in West Sussex. where a set of Chinese silver spoons could spell trouble for Jonty

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of the island in the Indian Ocean, off the southeastern coast of Africa. It has an ecosystem that has stood in isolation for millions of years, and produced an array of wildlife that cannot be found anywhere else on the planet. He begins by observing lemurs in their habitat, male red giraffe-necked weevils fighting each other, the courting rituals of chameleons, and the behaviour of spiders and fossas

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Newby Hall, North Yorkshire. Mark Stacey comes across two unusual tie and stick pins and Claire Rawle enthuses about a large collection of toy cars

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The daily struggles and dangers faced by a family of the animals living on the edge of the Kalahari desert. In the first episode, Shakespeare is bitten by a puff adder

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from day four of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. England captain Joe Root missed his side's First Test defeat in Southampton due to the birth of his second child, with Ben Stokes taking over as skipper at the Ageas Bowl. However, the Yorkshireman was back in action for the Second Test and - barring injury - was also expected to lead his team out in this final Test

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: Michael Ball looks at Victoria Wood's candid views on sex and relationships and her ability to say what many were thinking when it came to romance. Clips include stand up from the Albert Hall, spoof documentary, Mr Right, and moments from her sitcom, dinnerladies. With contributions from Duncan Preston, Anne Reid, Maxine Peake and Shobna Gulati. Plus, one of Victoria's most famous songs, The Ballad of Barry and Freda

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the student quiz's first-round matches, four students from Linacre College, Oxford pit their wits against a team from the Open University in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: The story of the battle for Fallujah is told through the eyewitness accounts of journalists, soldiers and civilians. Reporter Dexter Filkins and photographer Ashley Gilbertson were embedded with US Marines, with Gilberston revealing how the decision to capture an image of an Iraqi sniper changed not only his life but the lives of the soldiers with him. Fallujah resident Nidhal Abed talks about the fateful moment when she left her home to take her son to the doctors just a few streets away

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of guests Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Nish Kumar and Rachel Parris. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Sorry
programme.desc: Comedy short, written by and starring Lolly Adefope. An actress who considers herself on the verge of superstardom heads to the London premiere of her first Hollywood film. However, during the journey, she finds herself at the centre of a scandal on the internet that could destroy her career

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Britannia's Burning: Fire on the Bridge
programme.desc: Archive footage and eyewitness accounts are combined to tell the story of the blaze 50 years ago that ravaged the railway bridge over the Menai Straits connecting Anglesey and the mainland. The film also charts the construction of the original bridge and the deaths involved in its rebuilding as a modern road bridge

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap are in the Shropshire Hills to find out about an ambitious project to create a nature corridor between the two peaks of Long Mynd and Stiperstones. Tom meets a farmer who's become a convert to environmentally friendly regenerative farming, while Ellie goes in search of rare and much misunderstood wildlife. Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles returns with the programme in which three artists are challenged to create pieces for people they have never me, beginning by travelling to Shropshire to meet a buyer who wants to mark her recent return to her roots following a health scare

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Actress Felicity Montagu, musician Lady Leshurr, travel presenter Amar Latif, and TV presenters Gethin Jones and Jeff Brazier enter the studios to demonstrate their culinary skills. Gethin, Jeff and Lady Leshurr head to Pomana's, while Felicity and Amar cook for paying customers at Mortimer House Kitchen in a busy lunchtime service. Then it's back to the MasterChef kitchen to impress John and Gregg with their own creations. Who will have the culinary flair and skills to impress the judges?

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and John hires extra help for the lambing season, with New Zealander Emma helping a ewe to give birth to triplets and hoping the smallest and weakest one will survive. Martin invests in some hi-tech kit to alert him when his cows are calving and collects semen from his prize bull to sell, while Mel spends her birthday at a cattle auction

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actor Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who lived and painted on France's Cote d'Azur, renowned for its sunny climate. The first edition focuses on the relationship between modern art and the location's development as an international tourist haven. Cezanne, Monet and Renoir were captivated by the light and colour of the landscape in the 19th century and their resultant work advertised the region's beauty. Meanwhile, the area's vivid hues inspired Henri Matisse to adopt a new palette that set modern art on the route to abstraction

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: As the residents of Brizan gather on the beach for the annual surf party, a strange cloud suddenly falls over the ocean. When it retreats, 10 competing surfers have disappeared and only their empty and abandoned boards are pushed back by the waves onto the shore. Supernatural French drama, starring David Kammenos and Marie Dompnier

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The surviving surfers discover they are endowed with strange powers, while the cloud is about to appear again. Supernatural French drama, starring David Kammenos

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Having reigned over musical theatre for nearly five decades, Andrew Lloyd Webber's music has delighted millions worldwide in hit shows like Jesus Christ Superstar, Evita, Cats and Aspects of Love. In this programme, the composer chats to Alan Yentob about his autobiography Unmasked, a candid and confessional account of his early life and career up to the opening of The Phantom of the Opera in 1986

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and John hires extra help for the lambing season, with New Zealander Emma helping a ewe to give birth to triplets and hoping the smallest and weakest one will survive. Martin invests in some hi-tech kit to alert him when his cows are calving and collects semen from his prize bull to sell, while Mel spends her birthday at a cattle auction

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actor Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who lived and painted on France's Cote d'Azur, renowned for its sunny climate. The first edition focuses on the relationship between modern art and the location's development as an international tourist haven. Cezanne, Monet and Renoir were captivated by the light and colour of the landscape in the 19th century and their resultant work advertised the region's beauty. Meanwhile, the area's vivid hues inspired Henri Matisse to adopt a new palette that set modern art on the route to abstraction

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon conclude their exploration of Italy's capital. In the second of two programmes, as well as marvelling at the mosaics in the 12th-century Basilica di San Clemente, Andrew takes Giorgio to an ancient Roman schoolteacher's classroom. Then it's on to a true architectural and civic wonder - the vast Testaccio Slaughterhouse, where workers were once paid in offal. Travelling to the Palazzo Colonna, Andrew shows Giorgio the Beaneater by Carracci, a Baroque painting that makes an everyday subject extraordinary

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: From the 2012 Proms at London's Royal Albert Hall, conductor John Wilson and his orchestra are joined by the Maida Vale Singers to pay tribute to the composers and arrangers responsible for creating the Broadway Sound - among them such legendary tunesmiths as Jerome Kern, Cole Porter, Irving Berlin, George Gershwin, Frank Loesser and Leonard Bernstein. The concert includes show-stopping songs from Show Boat, On Your Toes, Brigadoon, Porgy and Bess, West Side Story and Fiddler on the Roof. Presented by Katie Derham

programme.title: Beethoven's Fidelio: The Royal Opera
programme.desc: Antonio Pappano conducts a production of Beethoven's only opera, Fidelio, from the Royal Opera House, a story of risk and triumph against the backdrop of revolution. David Butt Philip plays the political prisoner Florestan, and Lise Davidsen his wife Leonore - disguised as 'Fidelio' - who daringly sets out to save him from certain death. Tobias Kratzer's staging brings together the dark reality of the French Revolutionary 'Terror' and our own time to illuminate Fidelio's inspiring message of shared humanity

programme.title: Royal Opera House: The Re-Opening
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Katie Derham and Antonio Pappano introduce highlights of the Royal Opera House's first music and dance performances after lockdown, featuring a world premiere from choreographer Wayne McGregor, classic opera arias and song performed by Gerald Finley, Louise Alder, Toby Spence, David Butt Philip, Sarah Connolly and the Royal Opera House's Jette Parker Young Artists, plus excerpts from ballets by Ashton, MacMillan and Wheeldon. As its music director, Antonio is joined by fellow musicians from the Orchestra of the Royal Opera House and the performances are filmed in line with current social distancing rules against the backdrop of the venue's auditorium

programme.title: Sings Musicals
programme.desc: Memorable versions of songs taken from or inspired by musicals. The programme features Ella Fitzgerald singing Mack the Knife, from the Threepenny Opera, Captain Sensible's 1982 chart-topper Happy Talk, a cover of a song from South Pacific, and performances by Jeff Beck and Jay-Z

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat makes a discovery about her grandson, but it may be too late. Roxy and Peggy have a risky plan, and Sean finds a new enemy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Steven takes drastic action to protect his secret. Bianca and Ricky grow closer, and Clare's past catches up with her

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lucy faces a huge decision. Ricky thinks he is getting somewhere with Bianca, and Bradley is forced to act the hero

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is relieved to have his family back together. Roxy's visit to the doctor ends with more than a prescription, and Ricky and Bradley get surprising results from their endeavours

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Series in which the presenter travels around the country exploring the civilisation and culture of the ancient Egyptians. He begins by touring the spectacular underground tombs in the Valley of the Kings, where pharaohs were laid to rest. He gains access to the 150-room necropolis built for the sons of Rameses II, where he considers the skill of the vaults' designers, and goes in search of information on the labourers who slaved to build the palatial constructions

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: The Golden Age of Steam Railways
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the visionaries who rescued some of the narrow gauge railways that once served Britain's key industries, including its mines, quarries, factories and docks. Home movies reveal how volunteers set to work to bring the lines and steam engines back to life after they disappeared in the years following the Second World War. Narrated by Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: A look back at the glamorous heyday of the jet set, from the 1950s to the 1970s, examining a time when the notion of the rich and famous flying to exotic locations around the world was a source of fascination and admiration for the general public. Former racing driver Jackie Stewart, psychologist Dr Martin Dyer Smith, columnist Ross Benson and one-time Concorde pilot Christopher Orlebar are seen reflecting on society's attitude to celebrity

programme.title: Games Britannia
programme.desc: Benjamin Woolley traces the political and social impact that board games have had in Britain over the past 200 years, discovering how they were exported to the US and adapted to promote the American Dream. Monopoly celebrated wealth in the wake of the Great Depression, and together with Cluedo and Scrabble, formed a holy trinity of family favourites that endure to this day

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank charts the rise and fall of high-rise flats by following the history of one estate - the 19-storey Lincoln in Bow, east London, designed in 1960

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox concludes his examination of the three colours he considers most important to human history by arguing that white, traditionally a symbol of virtue and purity, was warped into a shade that exposed the darkest human instincts. He explores why the colour fascinated 18th-century potter Josiah Wedgwood and art historian Johann Joachim Winckelmann, and examines how in the 20th century it came to be linked with the Italian dictator Mussolini and fascism

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Series in which the presenter travels around the country exploring the civilisation and culture of the ancient Egyptians. He begins by touring the spectacular underground tombs in the Valley of the Kings, where pharaohs were laid to rest. He gains access to the 150-room necropolis built for the sons of Rameses II, where he considers the skill of the vaults' designers, and goes in search of information on the labourers who slaved to build the palatial constructions

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Carl's accordion routines are hindering George's puppet show rehearsals. George must decide whether to keep Carl and risk the show, or replace him and risk his friendship

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When the farmer gets stuck in the toilet, Bitzerhas to get vital supplies to him as a matter of urgency. His initial efforts fail and he drafts in Shaun and the gang to help out

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's over-reliance on gadgets prompts Colonel K to send his top agent on a tech-free nature retreat. Unbeknownst to Danger Mouse, Greenback has the same plans for his daughter, Delilah, whose social media obsession has also spiralled out of control. Back at HQ Penfold is tasked with protecting the entire world from the threat of super-villainy. Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. Soon enough, Delilah catches wind of DM's presence and she plots to destroy him as a means of getting back into her Dad's good books. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. They use the magic of television to get to the top of Wudang Mountain in China; taste Durian, the world's smelliest fruit, in Malaysia; head to Japan for some cosplay in Tokyo's Yoyogi Park; eat coconut curry for breakfast in India and take a tiny taxi called a Tuk-tuk for a treasure hunt challenge in Sri Lanka! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Inel Tomlinson, Hacker and Dodge, Naomi Wilkinson, Ben Shires, Victoria Cook and Michelle Ackerley - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place!

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Children organise a wedding for their unmarried parents or step-parents. Best friends Bella and Chloe plan a wedding of theatrical proportions as they put on a Shakespeare-inspired wedding. Mum and dad have to perform some of Shakespeare's most famous works for their wedding guests, while dodging rotten veg if their performance isn't up to scratch. With thunderous transport, outrageous acting and royal guests - this wedding is a full-on Shakespearean drama.

programme.title: Show Me What You're Made Of
programme.desc: Tom, Eloushka, Lucas, Maddie and Madz travel to a massive sandwich factory in London where they make seven hundred thousand sandwiches a week. Adelie Foods has very high hygiene standards and productivity rates and their workers are disciplined and incredibly fast. Will our five being able to demonstrate the focus needed to succeed here? When the boss starts them off making a cheese and pickle ploughman's sandwich, it becomes clear that this work is considerably more difficult than they had anticipated. As they all compete for a promotion, who has the skill to keep up with the production lines and make sandwiches of the quality that their boss expects?

programme.title: Bee Movie (2007)
programme.desc: A bee wearies of a life dedicated to producing honey and decides to leave his hive. During his foray into the wider world, he befriends a florist. However, when he realises what becomes of the honey his species toils so hard to make, he resolves to sue the entire human race for stealing from his fellow creatures. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jerry Seinfeld, Renee Zellweger and Chris Rock
