programme.desc: Huffty is not feeling well, so his friends try to cheer him up with games. However, his loud elephant sneezes keep interrupting - until Raa Raa thinks up a way to make his sneezes part of the fun

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Ruffy tries to learn how to ride a tricycle, but Timmy refuses to help

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: All the lights go out, so Dog and Pug explore all the night-time activities around them.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny hides Sula's sparkly shoe in the toilet when the pair want to carry on playing together, but his friend may have to hop home when the footwear gets wet

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos visit a skate park and practise turning on a skateboard. The Marching Band visits the Big Red Boat and takes turns turning with the Twirlywoos

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Using a sign language called Makaton, Something Special helps its audience learn how to communicate as well as develop their language in a fun and exciting way. Justin meets some musical friends and they put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of Tumbleton's Got Talent! starring Grandad Tumble, Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa makes up a song about all of the things she sees on her walk across Teletubbyland. Afterwards, she sings a song to Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po. They sing the song together and watch a children's choir sing in Tummy Tales

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with lots of hats and enjoy songs and story time together.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the Squirrels are out doing a bit of sailing. When Tag starts to feel a little bit seasick, Duggee suggests a stop off at the Island for a spot of lunch, but finds the picnic basket is empty. Everything is still on board and the boat is drifting out to sea, but thanks to Duggee having his Island Badge - he knows what to do

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: After spending the weekend at home doing chores, Wufflebump doesn't have any exciting Shriekend News to share with his classmates

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Rebecca steps through the Magic Curtain and goes to sea, hoping that she has what it takes to be the captain of an old-fashioned sailing boat

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a place where they'll make a nature collage using leaves and sticks. On the way, they discover a chocolate river and a bridge that looks like a sheep, choo-choo like an old train and even meet a dragon!

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Elephant has the hiccups, prompting the Patchwork Pals to try to find a way to help him

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ottalie's friend Noah comes to play. They spend the morning making an outside den with big sticks and blankets. Mum and little sister Imogen join them in the den for lunch. They also get creative and make a giant picture using lots of paint and sand

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: African folk tale about how the hyena got its short back legs

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Naima is cooking Algerian borek made from pastry, mashed potato, meat, cheese and olives

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells decorate Doug's bedroom. Waffle tries to help but causes a green paint disaster and escapes into Mrs Hobbs' house next door

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Yabba is given a drum to play, but Timmy is determined to exchange it for his triangle

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The Bubble Man arrives in town, so a visit to the Big Shop is in order for Sarah, Duck and Donkey. The adventures of a seven-year-old girl and her duck

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: It's a cold day and Tee wants to play outside. Mo agrees but only if he puts on enough layers...a fun dress up game ensues!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Fun and games with the 26 letters which come to life, tell stories and make words

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say banana and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see what bananas look and taste like. There are yummy bananas, mushy bananas and we all go bananas

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: When Nine needs a helping hand, he turns into a talented trio of circus blocks, the Three Threes. Plus, a look at how to share nine into equal parts

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing takes his Hoppity Voosh toy to the park, but accidentally leaves it behind. Amma helps him to focus so he can retrace his steps and locate the toy

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The fruit tea-loving bird-like characters, Great BigHoo, Toodloo, Chickedy and Chick, learn more about getting wet

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes ice skating with his friends and Mr Tumble builds a cosy den in his bedroom

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, forwards again!

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: When the Olobobs go out for a picnic, big clouds cover the sky and it starts raining

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The fox cub is getting ready to go to a wedding, but when he discovers a big purple creature with feathers on top of her head he wonders where his mum has gone

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Felix and Lily teach Hugo that he can use his imagination to work out difficult problems, and even help people too

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Maia has a big family weekend planned. First, she visits her baby cousin Jessica. They enjoy playing peek-a-boo together and Maia even helps to feed her. Later, Dad's family arrive for a traditional Zambian meal. Maia helps prepare the food with her Aunty and then they all dress up in special Zambian clothes to eat

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie shows off three amazing machines that are all made to race

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain's precious whistle gets blocked up and stops working, so Cook and Line try to replace it with other silly whistles

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels have had an exhausting morning playing. After their milk they can have their nap. However, Tag cannot sleep, so Duggee tries to help with his Sleeping Badge. He tells Tag about how other animals sleep and a few tricks they use to manage it

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Mr Bouncer invents something that seemingly has no specific function, and the rabbits try to find out what it is for

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how a suspension bridge works and finds out how playground flooring is made

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Everyone is practising their acts for the big talent show, but the curtain won't go up or down and Bob is feeling nervous about performing in front of everyone. So while Bitz tries to get the curtain working, Bob has to try to overcome his nerves and keep the audience happy with a song. Thankfully Purl is there to help and the show, and curtain, are a huge success

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Trouble brews in Jamaica when Glitch's campfire threatens a coffee plantation. Cartoon following four superheroes as they explore the world

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Moira brings Caitlin and Euan to the Kids Club, and Molly writes a poem

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is staying at Angela's kennels while the Brooklyn-Bells are on holiday.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go to the hairdresser's to see how Gran Gran gets her hair cut.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: After Sarah helps Scarf Lady prepare the wool for her new roof, she settles down for a dreamy sleep

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Lambkin must catch the tub wen it rolls away with Nana inside it

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small plant a little plant in Mother's garden. They get impatient for it to grow, but have little idea what they're letting themselves in for. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Everything in the garden is noisy, so Igglepiggle moves away to find a quiet place

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on plant-based food, cooking a 'buddha bowl' with onion bhajis, with vegetable grower Bryde Marshall and actress Jane McCarry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures, which include West Ham United v Aston Villa and Leicester City v Manchester United

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Gabby Logan look back at some of the most memorable moments by British athletes at Olympic Games, counting down the top 25 moments from Britain's Summer Olympics. Some of the highlights expected to feature are the battle between Seb Coe and Steve Cram in the 1500m at Los Angeles 1984, where Daley Thompson also made headlines in the Decathlon. Steve Redgrave's attempt to claim a fifth rowing gold medal at Sydney 2000 is sure to be high up the list, as are Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell's exploits in the men's 100m and women's 400m hurdles at Barcelona 1992. London 2012 saw Team GB claim a record number of gold medals, with sporting luminaries such as Bradley Wiggins, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Andy Murray and Mo Farah proving why they were household names, and new stars such as Nicola Adams, Laura Trott and Anthony Joshua burst onto the sporting landscape

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Comedian Paul Chuckle, actress Amanda Henderson, comic and writer Geoff Norcott and chef and entrepreneur Levi Roots tackle questions on English football clubs, songs from animated Disney films, the Star Wars prequel trilogy and reggae music

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Olympics edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Anthony Ogogo, Helen Glover, Sir Matthew Pinsent, James Cracknell, Nick Skelton, JJ Chalmers, Jeanette Kwakye and Mark Foster

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: With Lev acting distant, Faith struggles to cope whilst waiting for the results of Luka's latest scan. In a crisis, she calls Dylan, asking to see a friendly face, and he agrees to be there to support her. Meanwhile, Ethan bumps into Fenisha at Holby Comic Con and the pair are delighted to see each other. However, things soon turn awry when a van is accidently driven into a merchandise stall, seriously injuring several people, and Ethan is suddenly struck by terrifying memories of last year's terrorist attack

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Shane: For the Love of the Game
programme.desc: Documentary following Shane Williams as he attempts to build a life after rugby, going to work with the family firm and trying out other sports - and finding the more he tries to get away from the game, the more it gradually pulls him back onto the pitch

programme.title: Iron Man 3 (2013)
programme.desc: The armoured avenger hunts for the mastermind behind an untraceable series of bombings, despite being cut off from his usual resources and traumatised by a recent brush with death. His search uncovers a secret conspiracy involving a drug that renders the user indestructible. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Gwyneth Paltrow and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Glaswegian geek Milan snoops around the bedrooms of potential partners, hoping to meet a woman who loves the great outdoors and shares his love of Star Wars and superheroes

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sally Phillips and Angellica Bell take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of office space in Tring, Hertfordshire, a house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Lancashire and a semi-detached in Ambergate, Derby, after they are sold at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is on Crosby Beach celebrating the 15th anniversary of Antony Gormley's Another Place iron figure sculptures, takling to local Christians inspired by them to create their own artworks

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Amar Latif presents an appeal on behalf of Retina UK, a charity that provides support and information for those with inherited sight loss, and aslo funds research into potential treatments

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: A look back at the 1993 Five Nations championship match between Wales and England, which was held at Cardiff Arms Park

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards explores the history of the country, and begins by delving into the world's largest prehistoric copper mine and visiting an Iron Age hill fort. He also reveals the scale of the Roman occupation and explains how Welsh saints shared the gospel with the rest of the Celtic world

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen searches for clues about crime in Wales. He uncovers a story of canine cops, police badgers and vigilante ferrets, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad introduces reports and reaction from the final round of Premier League fixtures, as the 2019/20 top-flight season reaches its climax. With analysis from Garth Crooks and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani visit Chichester Harbour, the only Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in the UK that is managed by a harbour authority, discovering how the needs of a very busy harbour are balanced with those of nature and wildlife. While Matt is out on the water doing the daily rounds with the harbour master, Anita finds out about projects helping to protect local populations of oysters, terns and seals. Plus, Adam Henson's Highland bull Archie might not have long left the farm, but his legacy lives on

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire. Items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems, a Cartier clock, a tiny bowl with a giant price tag, a hat once given to Ronald Reagan and a walking stick belonging to Scottish music hall star Harry Lauder

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: In a newly independent India in 1951, passionate literature student Lata Mehra is looking towards her own future as she sets out to make her way in the world. She is not interested in romance, but her resolve is challenged when she starts to fall for a mysterious fellow student Kabir Durrani. Meanwhile, religious tensions mount between Muslims and Hindus in the wake of Partition, as India's first national democratic election approaches. Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the final round of Premier League fixtures, with the curtain coming down on the 2019-20 campaign almost 12 months after it began. European qualification and the relegation battle could both go down to the wire, with fixtures including Leicester City v Manchester United, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Ham United v Aston Villa. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles, Takumi, the Colonel and Alaska reunite for revenge after the Weekday Warriors go too far, while secrets are revealed and romance blossoms during a game of Best Day/Worst Day

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles and Lara are officially dating, and Alaska is not thrilled, while Sara warns the Colonel about the dire consequences of the prank they pulled on the Weekday Warriors

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the sales of a terrace house in Hornchurch, a piece of land in Newquay, and a commercial space in Rochdale

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A veteran racing driver is involved in a horrific crash on the finish line, two friends fish a hand grenade out of a river and a young boy alerts his mum to a car fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets an Action Man collector to learn more about this much-loved toy

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A revelation spreads through the residents, upsetting many and injuring relationships. Australian drama following life in an inner-city neighbourhood

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a home in the north Cornwall countryside with a budget of £525,000, and also visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion set out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield, but a messy encounter with a gull almost ruins one deal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Gillian Taylforth and Adam Woodyatt, aka mother and son Kathy and Ian Beale, who tell all about their unique working relationship and 35-year friendship. They talk about their biggest scenes - from Gillian's nerves while filming Kathy's shock return from the dead to the real reason Adam had to wear a hat throughout Ian's homeless storyline

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: A wildly successful music producer who is one of Speedi-Kazz's most loyal customers offers Kazim and Jeremiah the chance to use his studio for free - and they jump at the chance to record a promotional song for their delivery service

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Evan Biddell hosts as the contestants take on an eco-inspired design challenge, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: In the Dark Half (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager babysits a six-year-old neighbour, but is distraught when he unexpectedly dies in her care. While the dead child's father looks for someone to blame, she is tormented by the belief that his spirit is haunting the abandoned bunker she has made her refuge from her troubles. Horror, starring Jessica Barden, Tony Curran and Lyndsey Marshal

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is with a Norfolk father and daughter, who hope their £700k budget will afford them an equestrian property and annexe to house their family and horse

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley searching for three skip-bound items. Bruce Faulseit repurposes copper pipes, while Daniel Heath comes up with a radical redesign for two old tables

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock travel to Swindon, competing to transform an uninspiring garden into a welcoming and warm outdoor space, perfect for entertaining or dining

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Chester, where the buyer wants something to mark her 10th year of singing in an award-winning choir. With £500 on offer, will it be a glass maker who works on a barge, a collage artist who re-works magazines, or a textile worker of wool who makes the buyer's day? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad introduces reports and reaction from the final round of Premier League fixtures, as the 2019/20 top-flight season reaches its climax. With analysis from Garth Crooks and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani visit Chichester Harbour, the only Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in the UK that is managed by a harbour authority, discovering how the needs of a very busy harbour are balanced with those of nature and wildlife. While Matt is out on the water doing the daily rounds with the harbour master, Anita finds out about projects helping to protect local populations of oysters, terns and seals. Plus, Adam Henson's Highland bull Archie might not have long left the farm, but his legacy lives on

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire. Items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems, a Cartier clock, a tiny bowl with a giant price tag, a hat once given to Ronald Reagan and a walking stick belonging to Scottish music hall star Harry Lauder

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: In a newly independent India in 1951, passionate literature student Lata Mehra is looking towards her own future as she sets out to make her way in the world. She is not interested in romance, but her resolve is challenged when she starts to fall for a mysterious fellow student Kabir Durrani. Meanwhile, religious tensions mount between Muslims and Hindus in the wake of Partition, as India's first national democratic election approaches. Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the final round of Premier League fixtures, with the curtain coming down on the 2019-20 campaign almost 12 months after it began. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Gillian Taylforth and Adam Woodyatt, aka mother and son Kathy and Ian Beale, who tell all about their unique working relationship and 35-year friendship. They talk about their biggest scenes - from Gillian's nerves while filming Kathy's shock return from the dead to the real reason Adam had to wear a hat throughout Ian's homeless storyline

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: A wildly successful music producer who is one of Speedi-Kazz's most loyal customers offers Kazim and Jeremiah the chance to use his studio for free - and they jump at the chance to record a promotional song for their delivery service

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Evan Biddell hosts as the contestants take on an eco-inspired design challenge, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on plant-based food, cooking a 'buddha bowl' with onion bhajis, with vegetable grower Bryde Marshall and actress Jane McCarry

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures, which include West Ham United v Aston Villa and Leicester City v Manchester United

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Gabby Logan look back at some of the most memorable moments by British athletes at Olympic Games, counting down the top 25 moments from Britain's Summer Olympics. Some of the highlights expected to feature are the battle between Seb Coe and Steve Cram in the 1500m at Los Angeles 1984, where Daley Thompson also made headlines in the Decathlon. Steve Redgrave's attempt to claim a fifth rowing gold medal at Sydney 2000 is sure to be high up the list, as are Linford Christie and Sally Gunnell's exploits in the men's 100m and women's 400m hurdles at Barcelona 1992. London 2012 saw Team GB claim a record number of gold medals, with sporting luminaries such as Bradley Wiggins, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Andy Murray and Mo Farah proving why they were household names, and new stars such as Nicola Adams, Laura Trott and Anthony Joshua burst onto the sporting landscape

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Comedian Paul Chuckle, actress Amanda Henderson, comic and writer Geoff Norcott and chef and entrepreneur Levi Roots tackle questions on English football clubs, songs from animated Disney films, the Star Wars prequel trilogy and reggae music

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Olympics edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Anthony Ogogo, Helen Glover, Sir Matthew Pinsent, James Cracknell, Nick Skelton, JJ Chalmers, Jeanette Kwakye and Mark Foster

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: With Lev acting distant, Faith struggles to cope whilst waiting for the results of Luka's latest scan. In a crisis, she calls Dylan, asking to see a friendly face, and he agrees to be there to support her. Meanwhile, Ethan bumps into Fenisha at Holby Comic Con and the pair are delighted to see each other. However, things soon turn awry when a van is accidently driven into a merchandise stall, seriously injuring several people, and Ethan is suddenly struck by terrifying memories of last year's terrorist attack

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. programme.title: Iron Man 3 (2013)
programme.desc: The armoured avenger hunts for the mastermind behind an untraceable series of bombings, despite being cut off from his usual resources and traumatised by a recent brush with death. His search uncovers a secret conspiracy involving a drug that renders the user indestructible. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Gwyneth Paltrow and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Glaswegian geek Milan snoops around the bedrooms of potential partners, hoping to meet a woman who loves the great outdoors and shares his love of Star Wars and superheroes

programme.title: In the Dark Half (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager babysits a six-year-old neighbour, but is distraught when he unexpectedly dies in her care. While the dead child's father looks for someone to blame, she is tormented by the belief that his spirit is haunting the abandoned bunker she has made her refuge from her troubles. Horror, starring Jessica Barden, Tony Curran and Lyndsey Marshal

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sally Phillips and Angellica Bell take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of office space in Tring, Hertfordshire, a house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Lancashire and a semi-detached in Ambergate, Derby, after they are sold at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is on Crosby Beach celebrating the 15th anniversary of Antony Gormley's Another Place iron figure sculptures, takling to local Christians inspired by them to create their own artworks

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Amar Latif presents an appeal on behalf of Retina UK, a charity that provides support and information for those with inherited sight loss, and aslo funds research into potential treatments

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is with a Norfolk father and daughter, who hope their £700k budget will afford them an equestrian property and annexe to house their family and horse

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley searching for three skip-bound items. Bruce Faulseit repurposes copper pipes, while Daniel Heath comes up with a radical redesign for two old tables

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In the suburbs of Bristol, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich turn a long, pointed front garden into a water-saving, wildlife-friendly space

programme.title: Tide
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the science behind tides in coastal locations around the world, beginning with a look at how to judge when tides will rise and fall, a vital skill for ship's pilots, marine biologists and surfers. Featuring a visit to Canada's Bay of Fundy, where tourists flock to see one of the year's highest tides, and the Donegal coast, where champion surfer Dr Easkey Britton reveals how she learned to understand waves

programme.title: All That Lies Behind Me (Sgeulachd Donald Merrett)
programme.desc: Using dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion, John Morrison revisits the story of John Donald Merrett - a murderer who, having been cleared of killing his mother in 1927, spent the next 30 years on a spree across Europe, spending a large inheritance on women and the black market. The programme also reveals how when Merrett returned to Britain, he killed his wife and mother-in-law, before committing suicide

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Three months have passed and Màiri is still struggling to cope with the memory of the attack. A stranger arrives looking for Pàdruig causing more angst for Màiri

programme.title: Gaol @ Gael

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is thinks is out trick-or-treating for Halloween. But he unwittingly robs a bank instead of Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is a part-time firefighter reservist whose rescue mission for the day is to save a cat in a tree. In reality, the cat is none other than Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he is in the middle of a work-out at his local gym, Magoo inadvertently climbs onto a delivery boy's bicycle.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Thunderbirds discover that a mysterious villain is causing a series of underwater seaquakes. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak, Thomas Brodie-Sangster and David Graham

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz beams himself into Weasel's brain in order to take out Magoo with tranquilliser darts. Magoo loses Mr Cat and enlists his neighbour to find him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's in a triathlon and takes Fizz's superhuman strength headband, which Fizz needs to beat the President at a thumb war

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Today's the big day, and Magoo believes he is in a recording studio to make a hit single for famous producer, Phil Dustspeck. But is that really true?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is inspired by a movie he sees on TV and decides to direct his own film. He enlists the help of Iris, Fluffy and the turtles

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless hero visits the Science Museum, where he makes a new friend with a bagful of gadgets

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The ninja must break into the Explorer's Club to obtain the second Scroll of Forbidden Spinjitzu, running afoul of the club's manager and irate club-members.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Forever Knight finally assembles his Round Table

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: The Champs move to Ireland to save the family bed and breakfast, as the bank is threatening to sell and local character Bunni Shanahan is ready to take over

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guests Ade Adepitan, Raymond Blanc, Tom Kerridge and Dr Hazel Wallace

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare dishes perfect for enjoying outdoors, including spicy crab cake rolls served with thousand island dressing, cheese and sweet potato slices, classic donuts with both sweet and savoury fillings, homemade ginger beer and tropical lollies

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef ventures into Salem on the east coast of the USA, a place made famous by the witch trials of 1692. Things get spooky as James visits on the approach to Halloween and he meets a love clairvoyant, and her dogs

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.35 King George VI Stakes from Ascot, which is supported by four other races. Plus, four races from York, including the 2.40 Skybet Stakes. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the show's most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Fox, Ladybug, Flower, Tree and Rottweiler perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara present the final of the obstacle-course challenge. Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage, with those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: You Only Live Twice (1967)

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games, joined by singer-songwriter James Bay and actress Phyllis Logan

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare dishes perfect for enjoying outdoors, including spicy crab cake rolls served with thousand island dressing, cheese and sweet potato slices, classic donuts with both sweet and savoury fillings, homemade ginger beer and tropical lollies

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The baking expert prepares a selection of dough-based items, including breakfast bagels, doughnuts and cinnamon rolls. Master baker Richard Bertinet gives a lesson in baking sourdough, transforming it into a filled pain surprise, and Juliet also visits a mill to find out how flour is produced

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. The first edition celebrates snacks, visiting the Wagon Wheel factory in south Wales making millions of biscuits a year and the Melton Mowbray company responsible for an award-winning pork pie. Plus, how potatoes are taken from the field and turned into crisps in a matter of hours

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Wales is the only country in the world with a designated footpath that stretches around its entire coastline. Sean Fletcher sets out to explore the entire 870-mile Wales Coast Path, which runs from Chepstow in the south to Queensferry in Flintshire in the north. His first leg takes Sean along the coast of north Wales and on to the island of Anglesey. Along the way searches for Britain's rarest toad, turns his hand to making a Bronze Age axe head and has a beach cookout using mussels and oysters caught fresh from the Menai Strait

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Fox, Ladybug, Flower, Tree and Rottweiler perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Paul O'Grady for the Love of Dogs: What Happened Next
programme.desc: The comedian catches up with some of the most memorable dogs he has met from the eight years he has spent at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home, and learns how their lives have been transformed since leaving the shelter. In the first episode, there is a surprising revelation about Tulip, the puppy Paul fell in love with five years ago. Elsewhere, Paul discovers the uplifting story of Bruce, a terrified puggle who has gone on to change the lives of his new family

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates an edition that trawls the archives of the soap to look back at some of the most notorious baddies to ever walk the streets of Weatherfield, featuring the likes of Richard Hillman, Charlie Stubbs, Maya Sharma and Pat Phelan

programme.title: Live and Let Die (1973)
programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the murder of three British agents leads him to a connection between the dictator of a Caribbean island and a notorious Harlem drug kingpin - along with a plot to flood America with limitless supplies of free heroin. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore in his first appearance as 007, alongside Yaphet Kotto and Jane Seymour

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Leslie falls in with a bad crowd and ends up being falsely accused of armed robbery, prompting Louisa to take desperate measures to save him. Margo takes a shine to a gardener at the Villa Mavrodaki, Captain Creech returns, and the family realises the extent of Gerry's passion for animals when he turns his bedroom into a centre for scientific learning. Guest starring Leslie Caron

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mr Carson and Mrs Hughes' wedding is fast approaching, but a misunderstanding threatens to derail the occasion, and when the big day arrives it also brings a few unexpected guests. Meanwhile, Edith reconnects with an old acquaintance, who comes to her aid in her hour of need, and over at the Dower House, Spratt has something to hide, meaning Denker is hot on his trail. Daisy jumps to conclusions about one of the countess's plots, and tensions mount over the hospital takeover, prompting Isobel to offend a friend. Period drama, starring Jim Carter, Phyllis Logan, Elizabeth McGovern and Laura Carmichael

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tom and Sybbie return to Downton, but the peace is disrupted by an unexpected visit from an old friend. Baxter is faced with a dilemma after an encounter with Sergeant Willis, while Thomas struggles to fill Carson's shoes. Anna goes to London with Mary to deal with a crisis, while her secrets seem likely to be exposed. Daisy is not happy with Cora, and Mary has an admirer

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past, including Marc Wolfe's struggle to form a bond with his birth mother Esther, who lives in New Zealand. However, his search for identity didn't end there as researchers were able to reunite him with his birth father David, and the pair have grown close. One significant hurdle remained in the form of introducing David to his adoptive father Terry, and cameras were there as Marc introduces his two dads to each other

programme.title: Gone to Pot: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Birds of a Feather's Linda Robson, ex-EastEnders star Pam St Clement, actor Christopher Biggins, retired footballer John Fashanu and darts pundit Bobby George go on a road trip across America to explore the pros and cons of legalising marijuana. They examine its use for both medical and recreational purposes in US states where it is legal and meet people who have experienced the benefits and the downsides

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Robert Rinder hears the case of a man claiming £328 for two golf clubs that he lent to a friend and were subsequently stolen

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Blackpool, where Debbie Serpell tries to buy some Moorcroft pottery, James Layte has his eye on a power hammer and a selection of football medals look like being a bargain find. But which will the dealers clinch and which will go under the hammer?

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five Manchester market workers answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz hosted, by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Wendy, Alfred and Frances pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Drama unfolds in the garage, Cain and Moira grow closer, and Charity is frustrated by another dead end

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen wakes up after a heart attack, and Brian is annoyed that Bernie found a watch in the woods and sold it to Sarah. Meanwhile, Abi tells Ed that Aggie can use Kevin's car until hers is fixed as the neighbourhood is grateful for putting herself on the front line at the hospital

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's book group discusses Crime and Punishment at the house, which turns out to be surprisingly appropriate after Tracey warns Garth not to lead his younger brother astray. This episode features the return of Melanie Fishman, Dorien's rival from the original BBC series. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Jan Goodman star

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look at the classic pairings and comedy double acts from the soap that have kept viewers entertained through the decades, including Stan and Hilda Ogden, Fred Elliott and Ashley Peacock, and Steve McDonald and Lloyd Mullaney.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: After weeks of speculation, the Broadchurch community struggles to come to terms with the truth about the events surrounding Danny Latimer's death, and the revelations threaten to prevent the dead boy from being allowed to rest in peace. Crime drama, starring David Tennant, Olivia Colman, Andrew Buchan, Jodie Whittaker, Arthur Darvill, Vicky McClure and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Goals
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef and food writer Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to authentic Indian food, sharing her family's own recipes and visiting cooks across the UK and various regions of India. In the first episode, Parveen shares a family recipe for lamb bhuna with raita and rice and shares thoughts on keeping spices fresh. She also takes a trip to Delhi, sampling some traditional breakfast dishes and meeting the acclaimed chef behind some groundbreaking street food

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is thinks is out trick-or-treating for Halloween. But he unwittingly robs a bank instead of Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is a part-time firefighter reservist whose rescue mission for the day is to save a cat in a tree. In reality, the cat is none other than Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he is in the middle of a work-out at his local gym, Magoo inadvertently climbs onto a delivery boy's bicycle.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Thunderbirds discover that a mysterious villain is causing a series of underwater seaquakes. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak, Thomas Brodie-Sangster and David Graham

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz beams himself into Weasel's brain in order to take out Magoo with tranquilliser darts. Magoo loses Mr Cat and enlists his neighbour to find him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's in a triathlon and takes Fizz's superhuman strength headband, which Fizz needs to beat the President at a thumb war

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Today's the big day, and Magoo believes he is in a recording studio to make a hit single for famous producer, Phil Dustspeck. But is that really true?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is inspired by a movie he sees on TV and decides to direct his own film. He enlists the help of Iris, Fluffy and the turtles

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless hero visits the Science Museum, where he makes a new friend with a bagful of gadgets

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The ninja must break into the Explorer's Club to obtain the second Scroll of Forbidden Spinjitzu, running afoul of the club's manager and irate club-members.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Forever Knight finally assembles his Round Table

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: The Champs move to Ireland to save the family bed and breakfast, as the bank is threatening to sell and local character Bunni Shanahan is ready to take over

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guests Ade Adepitan, Raymond Blanc, Tom Kerridge and Dr Hazel Wallace

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare dishes perfect for enjoying outdoors, including spicy crab cake rolls served with thousand island dressing, cheese and sweet potato slices, classic donuts with both sweet and savoury fillings, homemade ginger beer and tropical lollies

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef ventures into Salem on the east coast of the USA, a place made famous by the witch trials of 1692. Things get spooky as James visits on the approach to Halloween and he meets a love clairvoyant, and her dogs

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.35 King George VI Stakes from Ascot, which is supported by four other races. programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals

programme.desc: Five Manchester market workers answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz hosted, by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Wendy, Alfred and Frances pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Drama unfolds in the garage, Cain and Moira grow closer, and Charity is frustrated by another dead end

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen wakes up after a heart attack, and Brian is annoyed that Bernie found a watch in the woods and sold it to Sarah. Meanwhile, Abi tells Ed that Aggie can use Kevin's car until hers is fixed as the neighbourhood is grateful for putting herself on the front line at the hospital

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's book group discusses Crime and Punishment at the house, which turns out to be surprisingly appropriate after Tracey warns Garth not to lead his younger brother astray. This episode features the return of Melanie Fishman, Dorien's rival from the original BBC series. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Jan Goodman star

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look at the classic pairings and comedy double acts from the soap that have kept viewers entertained through the decades, including Stan and Hilda Ogden, Fred Elliott and Ashley Peacock, and Steve McDonald and Lloyd Mullaney.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: After weeks of speculation, the Broadchurch community struggles to come to terms with the truth about the events surrounding Danny Latimer's death, and the revelations threaten to prevent the dead boy from being allowed to rest in peace. Crime drama, starring David Tennant, Olivia Colman, Andrew Buchan, Jodie Whittaker, Arthur Darvill, Vicky McClure and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Goals

programme.desc: Chef and food writer Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to authentic Indian food, sharing her family's own recipes and visiting cooks across the UK and various regions of India. She also takes a trip to Delhi, sampling some traditional breakfast dishes and meeting the acclaimed chef behind some groundbreaking street food

programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The killing of a postman, whose doorstep deliveries often included more than just letters, rocks the idyllic rural hamlet of Goodman's Land and sends Barnaby on the trail of a murderer with vengeance in mind - if Sgt Troy can keep his eyes off the attractive village policewoman long enough to follow the clues. Murder mystery, guest starring Robert Glenister, with John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Gillian Kearney

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An arson attack on a Turkish social club leaves 17 people dead and Brooks, Devlin and Chandler fearing the crime was racially motivated. They uncover a trail of false identities, but face opposition from a respected barrister when they need to remove a key piece of evidence from a suspect's leg. Meanwhile, Steel is forced to confront one of his oldest friends. Guest starring Charles Kay, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. The story shocks the detectives and their boss Chandler as they investigate a homophobic religious group at the centre of the incident involving members of their own force. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster leaves central Manchester, heading east along the Rochdale Canal. Not long into his journey, John finds himself struggling to contend with 19th-century technology at a cotton works. Further up the canal, he helps out members of an conservation group clearing the waterway of dumped supermarket trollies. After passing into West Yorkshire, it's on to Hebden Bridge, famous for the hard-wearing material corduroy, before John tries his hand at clog-making in one of Britain's few remaining factories dedicated to the craft
