programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Beverly signs everyone up for a holiday family photo, Geoff is excited to join in, despite Erica's reservations. Barry must face the fact that he has not been a good friend

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Feeling pressured to beef up the contents of his CV, Adam decides to try out for William Penn's pickleball team. Beverly is determined to throw Murray an epic birthday party

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three Essex couples compete for the cash prize of £1,000 with Kellie and Scott, Stuart and Jamie, and Nick and Lisa taking turns to try to make the best impression. Married couple Kellie and Scott kick-off with a love-themed menu, but decorating company owner Stuart and his lady-of-leisure wife Jamie are shocked to see that the menu consists of not one, but two nearly identical dishes to their own menu

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Adrenaline junkie groom-to-be Jai plans on having a wedding in the sky, neglecting the fact that his fiancee Leanne is terrified of heights

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: As the end of the experiment nears its end, the grooms are given one last chance to woo their brides on an epic final date. For some the pressure of their upcoming vow renewals is taking its toll and it remains to be seen which of the couples will crumble under the thought of life outside of the experiment

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel becomes frustrated when Joe's demanding new job prevents the pair spending time together. She sneaks over to see him at work, but runs the risk of getting him fired. Meanwhile, Ryder witnesses a robbery and discovers that his interview with the local news team has been turned into a video that has gone viral. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jeffords' favourite author, DC Parlov, gets in touch after his latest manuscript is stolen, so Jeffords and Peralta convince Diaz to go undercover with them at a fantasy fiction convention to find the culprit. Back at the precinct, Holt, Santiago and Boyle take a forensics course, but Boyle's big mouth gets him in trouble

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: While the squad attends a funeral in Los Angeles, Captain Holt learns that he is in the running for his dream job: Commissioner of the NYPD. however, after they miss their return flight to New York, Peralta must find a way to get Holt back in time for his interview. Santiago tries to convince everyone that she can be laid back, Jeffords panics about losing his first-class flight, and Boyle pries into Diaz's personal life

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: A worried Mary sends Sheldon off to Sunday school after she finds him playing Dungeons and Dragons with Tam and Billy, but Sheldon is soon inspired to study a variety of religions. US comedy, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The youngster asks for a computer and unknowingly sparks a heated argument between his parents over the family's finances, prompting Mary and the twins to move in with Meemaw. Iain Armitage stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny runs into money troubles, so Sheldon offers to lend her some cash to help her pay the rent. However, when Leonard discovers his neighbour's ex-boyfriend Kurt is the cause of her financial woes, he gathers the gang for a confrontation. Starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny share an intimate moment as they recover from a psychological deconstruction by the physicist's mother, an expert in psychiatry and neural sciences. Guest starring Mamma Mia! actress Christine Baranski

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel and Joe's friendship with Nate and Gillian is jeopardised when a secret is revealed, and Zander begs Ryder to let him move into the basement without Lennox's knowledge, after he drops out of art school. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox secretly dates her teaching assistant, and when Joe and Mel find out, they try to put an end to the relationship - only things do not go according to plan. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam abandons Pops to go trick-or-treating with some new friends, but he gets a shock when he discovers what they are really planning. Meanwhile, Beverly dons a costume so she can attend a teenager's Halloween party in secret and protect Barry from social humiliation. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry is thrilled by the freedom his new driving licence brings, until Beverly insists he sticks to her 10 rules of the road at all times. Meanwhile, Pops refuses to wear an emergency alert bracelet and is forced to call Adam when he suffers a fall. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When a fire destroys Boyle's food truck, Pimento unexpectedly shows up at the scene, working as an insurance investigator. Diaz helps Santiago to see that it's fine to get excited about picking her wedding dress, while Gina and Jeffords coach Captain Holt in the art of social media. Comedy, starring Joe Lo Truglio and Jason Mantzoukas

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle organises an elaborate scavenger hunt for Peralta's bachelor party, but the guys take some short cuts that throw the event into disarray. Santiago's bachelorette party goes awry when it comes to light that she once slept with a member of the band that is to play at her wedding reception

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: While Sheldon is busy obsessing over the status of Meemaw's budding relationship with his mentor Dr John Sturgis, George and Mary attempt to bond with their other children. Comedy spin-off prequel to The Big Bang Theory, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: Meemaw is pursued by two gentlemen callers, only for Sheldon to interfere, forcing her to set some ground rules. Richard Kind and Wallace Shawn guest star, with Iain Armitage

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon returns to Texas in disgrace when he discovers his friends tampered with the Arctic expedition data after growing tired of his quest for a scientific breakthrough. Elsewhere, Leonard becomes frustrated as constant interruptions hamper his attempts to woo Penny

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Howard stake their most coveted comic books on a bet over the species of a cricket, while Leonard and Penny struggle to recover from an awkward first night together. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons, Simon Helberg and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: Hollyoaks Favourites
programme.desc: Showcasing five of the soap's most talked-about storylines. Jennifer Metcalfe and Nick Pickard introduce the first of five episodes this week originally aired in the period from 1995 to 2000, beginning with one from 1999. Villain Rob Hawthorne seeks revenge on his former lover Lucy Benson by kidnapping her during her brother Kurt's funeral. Plus, Max doesn't know how to tell his mum Angela that Cindy's boyfriend is a danger to Holly

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The couples go on their final dates before the renewal of their vows. Nadia questions Anthony's integrity, while Simon and Alene reveal that they more than like each other

programme.title: Supernanny US
programme.desc: Ben Corry is away for months at a time with the army, leaving stay-at-home mum Maria to discipline their four rowdy daughters. Jo Frost brings this military family into line

programme.title: Celebs Go Virtual Dating
programme.desc: Famous faces vet potential matches via video before going on socially distanced dates, featuring Geordie Shore star Chloe Ferry, rugby union player and Celebrity X Factor star Levi Davis, TOWIE heart-throb Pete Wicks and Love Island's Shaughna Phillips

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Reformed teen rebel and unlikely baker Sunny, 33, is partnered with Misha, but is a cupcake enough to spark a romance between these two self-professed boffins? In a first for the show, 22-year-old twins nervously embark on a double date, with die-hard Cher fan Stephen meeting legal assistant Emily, while his sister Rebecca sits down with engineer Kristian. Plus, there is a special guest at the First Dates restaurant as maitre d' Fred's father visits from France. And naturally, he has a way with the ladies

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Buffy starts to lose her powers as her 18th birthday approaches. She remains unaware that Giles is responsible, having been ordered by the Watchers' council to put her through a horrific rite of of passage. She must be reduced to a mere human and sent into a battle to the death with an insane vampire. However, neither Giles nor the bloodsucker are prepared to follow the Watchers' rules

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Xander's self esteem hits rock bottom - he gets no respect around school and fears he contributes nothing to fighting the forces of evil. He gets the chance to be a hero during a wild night involving fast cars, faster women, a gang of reanimated corpses on a crime spree and a spot of DIY bomb disposal - all the while unaware his friends are busy averting the apocalypse without him

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends try to solve the mystery of Sheldon's post-lunch activities, following him to a secret room where they discover how he chills out. Elsewhere, Leonard is jealous when Penny starts texting a classmate, but things quickly change when Alex gets flirty with him

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon feels undermined when his parking space at the university is allocated to Howard instead, and his determination to cling on to the spot causes a rift in the pair's friendship. However, the battle between the boys pales in comparison to that waged by Amy and Bernadette, whose disagreement on behalf of their significant others becomes increasingly personal. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt investigates concerns about levels of naturally occurring arsenic in baby rice products, Jimmy Doherty examines Marks & Spencer's claim that they can trace all their beefburgers back to the cow, and Kate Quilton find out whether a parasite could be hiding in sushi. Programme exploring the secrets behind mass-produced food, meeting technicians, scientists, factory owners, growers and producers to find out exactly what goes into the groceries that end up on the supermarket shelves

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: London couple Bob and Annette Roberts have been holidaying in Grenada for 25 years and now, with retirement on the horizon, they're looking to invest £225,000 in a holiday home. Adrian Simpson shows them round a selection of what is available on the market

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Adrian Simpson shows Deborah and Peter Merritt a selection of properties for sale in Tobago. The couple downsized in the UK, and have decided to invest £300,000 in a holiday home on the breathtaking Caribbean island close to Trinidad

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Adrian Simpson meets Colin and Veronica Moore, who want a holiday home in St Lucia where they can escape the British winters, so he searches the Caribbean island for properties on a budget of £200,000

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Bath, Kirstie Allsopp meets first time buyers Laura and Phil, who are desperate to make the move from shared living to a home of their own. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer goes property searching with downsizing duo Kerrie and Dave, who sold their beloved barn conversion to move closer to their kids

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The semi-finals have arrived, with four teams battling for a place in the final, with the challenges including five hours to create a magical Charlotte Russe display, while at the same time preparing all of the elements needed to perform a spectacular, World of Magic-inspired piece of gourmet theatre, known as live plating. At the end of the challenge, the teams have just three minutes to transform their table into a gastronomic work of illusion and theatre, under the tough gaze of judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gemma Willock hosts the first party in the West Midlands, and her evening of glitz and glamour looks set to be a roaring success until an unwelcome revelation about her corner-cutting approach to the competition threatens her chances of taking the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mortgage adviser Andrew Devey throws the second party in the West Midlands, hosting a beatnik-themed evening. He cooks shepherd's pie for his main course, as well as lining up some pop art in the hope that it will expand his guests' minds

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jules Evans hosts the third day of the competition in the West Midlands, and plans to indulge her passion for historical re-enactments by planning a medieval-themed dinner. The guests offer mixed responses when they look at the menu, but things take a turn for the better when she announces a duel will form part of the evening's entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Massage therapist Lucy Hird hosts the fourth dinner party from the West Midlands, featuring an extensive menu full of Thai specialities. She also gives individual presents to her guests, but her night is compromised by the entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mental-health nurse Lloyd Cast hosts the final dinner party from the West Midlands. Despite appearing to be an extrovert so far, he decides on a chilled theme for the evening to allow his food to take centre stage, before the winner of the £1,000 is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Golf House on the Isle of Wight, where hosts John and Sue Blakemore hope to impress their guests during their stay. Things start off positively with a well-received breakfast, but a suspicious stain in a toilet causes controversy among some of the competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to St John's Wood, north-west London, where Nick Tyrie and his friend Layla Amini welcome their guests to the New Inn Pub. One of the proprietors tries to win points by revealing her musical past, but John and Sue Blakemore feel their room is too impersonal, and are underwhelmed by the choices at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Bunkroom hostel in Chester, David Sharrock is determined to prove that hostelling is for everybody - even if his wife Victoria isn't convinced. It's bunkbeds and budget breakfasts for all, as his fellow hosts decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group heads to John Graham's the B&B in Blackpool for the final visit of the week, and an afternoon of tenpin bowling brings out the guests' competitive sides. Later, there is more tension at the dinner table when the host is subjected to aggressive questioning by John and Sue Blakemore

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the guest house owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service that they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winner is announced

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It

Christian dreams of a home bursting with character, but Lauren thinks that they can refurbish their four-bedroom detached property to make it more suitable for them and their two small children

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients dealt with at King's College Hospital, London include a man who was stabbed in his own home and has injuries to his lung, arm and liver, and an elderly woman injured in a fall. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 77-year-old former motorbike racer arrives with a potentially deadly swelling of the main artery that could burst at any moment. His only option is major surgery, which could be dangerous at his age. A woman whose boyfriend was killed in a cycling accident a year previously is also injured while on her bike, and a medical technician and a mobile caterer talk about their roles in aiding people to recovery

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Thoroughly Modern Millican Live
programme.desc: Stand-up performance, recorded at London's Hammersmith Apollo during the comedienne's 2012 UK tour. Anecdotes include life's humdrum curiosities, the wonders of making a home-made treadmill, the reasons for carrying spare pants and the practicalities of an orgy

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend travel to Cornwall in a bid to restore a run-down Triumph Herald that has been housed in a garage for five years

programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kennels owner Dean Faulkner is keen to impress as he hosts the first dinner party in Cambridge, where his guests are beauty school co-ordinator Kay Greenwood, gardening enthusiast Matt Sullivan, headstrong international development manager Hawa Sydique and the Beatles-obsessed Dave Goodier

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty school co-ordinator Kay Greenwood hosts the second dinner party in Cambridge, hoping her tempura prawns and beef stroganoff will win her the £1,000 prize. One guest is sceptical about her chances of success, but changes his mind when he samples her unique entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gardening enthusiast Matt Sullivan hosts the third dinner party in Cambridge, where his home-grown English menu of 'posh' fish and chips and rice pudding surprises his guests. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: International development manager Hawa Sydique hosts the fourth dinner party from Cambridge, and her adventurous menu seems to cause more problems than it is worth. However, she hopes to impress with the 20-piece samba band in her living room, but the other guests are a little overwhelmed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beatles-loving Scouser David Goodier hosts the final dinner party in Cambridge and is determined to impress his guests with a 1960s Merseybeat dress code and courses entitled 'Spicy Beatballs' and 'Tir-Mersey-su'. As the evening draws to a close, the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Ye Olde Fighting Cocks in Arnside, Cumbria, where owners Tony Keates and Jeanette Hamer hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money. However, while the B&B's guests are bowled over by the spectacular views, they are less than impressed by Tony's tales of business success and a mouldy mattress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Hadleigh, Suffolk, where Dr Stephen Stanley-Little invites fellow B&B owners to come and stay at his tranquil country setting - Westward Cottage. He takes his guests on a historic village tour that leaves one couple plotting their escape, while the host's relaxed approach to breakfast drives some of the others mad

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host are bohemian couple Avril Berry and Alan Fraser of Roulotte Retreat in Melrose in the Scottish Borders. Their unique gypsy caravans are not to everyone's liking, and a yoga class fails to calm the tensions that seem to be rising within the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Elgin Hotel, Blackpool, where manager Garry Lloyd and his daughter Sophie Seddon want to demonstrate how their 89-room business can still deliver a personal touch. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five amateur cooks from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, take turns trying to throw the perfect dinner party. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT expert and disco fanatic Joel Rowbottom throws the second of the week's parties in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with an exciting mixture of food and dance. The host faces awkward questions about his disco pants, resulting in some frank revelations, but hopes he can turn the evening's fortunes around with his 'dad dancing' competition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Donna Stephenson hopes to impress with a main course of 'bunny delight' as she hosts the third dinner party from Wakefield, West Yorkshire - but her guests are distracted by the number of animals she has

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shoeless window cleaner Jake Coxon hosts the fourth dinner party from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with beef Wellington, and performing his song God Is a Rocking Chair with his band the Sourheads

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Francesca Saynor hosts the final dinner party in Wakefield, hoping that her simple menu and back-to-school theme will be enough to impress her guests and make her the winner of the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon to meet Kevin McCabe, a man who wanted to build one of the biggest houses the programme has ever seen. Finally, seven years after it began, the herculean task of building this giant cob castle is complete. Has it proven to be the great home he originally intended, and does it prove as he hoped that cob is a viable material for contemporary architecture?

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A look at some of the abuse paramedics have to deal with, including a man - thought to be having a fit - who verbally abuses and spits at the paramedics who are trying to help him. There is also a call regarding a baby in cardiac arrest, which actually turns out to be a hoax

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: Devon and Cornwall Police resource deployment officer JJ must prioritise resources for the most dangerous domestic calls, complex and serious crimes and mental health incidents on the basis of threat, risk and potential harm. Response officer Amie and her partner search the cliffs of Devon for a missing person, while force Incident manager Martin dispatches specialist teams to Cornwall to deal with a potential armed siege

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the importance of the father-son relationship and the potential consequences and difficulties when this relationship is not a positive one. Frontline officers talk frankly about their experiences of how the breakdown of this pivotal formative experience can lead to young men perpetuating cycles of violence, lacking confidence, or struggling with mental health problems

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's in Plainfield, New Jersey, but struggles to get his message across to indomitable owner Shelley. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael locate their heating problem, but it means that Billy - who's scared of height - has to repoint the chimney 20 metres up. programme.title: Four in a Bed

The guests get competitive in a round of clay shooting, and debate whether they believe in the existence of ghosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Cranleigh House B&B in Combe Martin, Devon, where former couple Katherine Armitage and Stewart Johnston offer alternative accommodation for those who enjoy yoga and are seeking spiritual enlightenment. They hope the group will send out energy for world peace and healing, but Mike and Denise question whether the whole thing is a joke

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike and Denise Mitchell welcome the contestants to Beaches B&B in Paignton, Devon, which only opened for business a few weeks ago. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together for one last time to discover what they have been paid, and the identity of the winner. Katherine and Stuart defend their vegetarian breakfast, and Mike and Denise deal with negative comments about their cereal selection

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: With numbers in the wild dwindling rapidly, Chester Zoo's black rhino breeding programme is a key part of preserving the species. Black rhino Kitani is introduced to new mate Magadi, but with injury a high risk during mating, keeper Babs is concerned that Kitani may struggle to defend herself, since she lost her horn after the death of her calf. Meanwhile, there's a surprise birth in the orangutan house, and older sibling Tuti has to fend for herself and set out on the road to independence

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Great Dane is rushed to Fitzpatrick Referrals after losing the use of its back legs due to a slipped disc, with complications leaving the dog fighting for its life. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: Grand Designs

programme.title: Not Going Out

programme.title: Last Stop Garage

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef takes a gastronomic road trip to Istanbul through the countries of the former Byzantine Empire - a melting pot of East and West. His journey begins in the city of Venice, and on the idyllic Greek island of Symi he cooks some of his favourite Venetian dishes including seafood risotto, tiramisu and gnocchi with spider crab

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three motoring enthusiasts head to Spain on a mission to help its struggling economy - but the convertible supercars they take are very expensive and anything but discreet. With Jeremy Clarkson in a McLaren MP4-12C, Richard Hammond driving a Ferrari 458 and James May behind the wheel of an Audi R8 V10, the trio encounter mountain passes, empty towns and an abandoned airport before creating their own street circuit in a deserted Madrid development. Plus, Sherlock star Benedict Cumberbatch sets a lap time in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a project to build a car that can turn into a hovercraft and bring relief to flooded areas. Plus, Jeremy grabs the keys to two variants of the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG supercar - the V8-engined Black Series with a roaring 622bhp, and the 740bhp Electric Drive, which is powered by four electric motors. programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to the south-west corner of Wales and one of Britain's finest coastal paths to find out why Pembrokeshire feels so English, even though it's more than 100 miles from the border. With its numerous castles and a fine cathedral, there are sure signs the Normans stamped their mark over the area. Tony's four-day walk traces the story of their conquest of the county, which he discovers was very different from the knockout victory at Hastings, involving a long, fiercely fought struggle

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies
programme.desc: Studio and concert performance from the decade, featuring the Who, Queen, David Bowie, the Moody Blues, the Rolling Stones and Elton John

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the connections between the wildlife, landscape and people of the Arabian peninsula. The first edition features animals including oryxes, jerboas, horned vipers and scorpions, and a journey with the Bedouin - camel-riding nomads who are the only humans to have mastered the art of survival in the desert. Narrated by Alexander Siddig

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Exploring the mountains of Dhofar in Oman, where the annual Indian Ocean monsoon has helped create a lost world of waterfalls and cloud forests filled with fascinating wildlife. The programme shows green sea turtles coming ashore in their thousands, shadowed by egg-stealing foxes, as well as footage of striped hyenas doing battle with Arabian wolves. Plus, a glimpse into the lives of chameleons, honey badgers and the elusive Arabian leopard

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: The changes that have swept across the Arabian peninsula since the discovery of oil. Camel racing has become a hi-tech sport and the region's animals now live alongside a very modern society, but technology is also being used to protect nature. The programme reveals how dugongs are fitted with satellite transmitters and hunting falcons chase down radio-controlled planes, and follows the construction of the world's first carbon-neutral city. Narrated by Alexander Siddig

programme.desc: Crimes involving members of royal families from around the globe, beginning with an earl shot dead in a hedonistic hideaway for colonial aristocrats in Kenya

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline
programme.desc: The highs and lows of Prince Harry and Prince William's roles in the forces, featuring an interview with Harry in which he discusses a planned secret deployment in Afghanistan

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Within a dense Polish woodland sits a forgotten complex with an explosive history. Also, grand coastal defences in Ghana reveal a tortured past

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to the south-west corner of Wales and one of Britain's finest coastal paths to find out why Pembrokeshire feels so English, even though it's more than 100 miles from the border. With its numerous castles and a fine cathedral, there are sure signs the Normans stamped their mark over the area. Tony's four-day walk traces the story of their conquest of the county, which he discovers was very different from the knockout victory at Hastings, involving a long, fiercely fought struggle

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: The Allies reached the last great natural barrier between their forces and the German heartland. If they could get across the River Rhine, the war would be won

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Documentary explaining how a squadron of low-flying bombers freed resistance fighters from Amiens Jail during the Second World War

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: First hand accounts of America's opening air raid on the Japanese archipelago, led by Colonel Jimmy Doolittle

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Stories of people who came close to losing their lives during the Second World War, beginning with tales about the Chindits, the army's elite jungle troops division who beat the Japanese in a daring mission

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The bravery of the 333rd Field Artillery Battalion, a depleted unit of African-American soldiers who risked everything to fight off the Nazis at Bastogne, Belgium

programme.desc: Rene reluctantly assists the Resistance as they set out to blow up a railway line, but the mission is jeopardised when Edith mistakes the explosives for gin. Meanwhile, a plane from London supposedly transporting German uniforms turns out to contain a tailor

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: While on a sabotage mission, Rene is captured by General Von Klinkerhoffen, who promptly orders the Colonel to inflict drastic punishment on him - but rather than going through with the grisly event, he arranges to fake the cafe owner's death instead

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene, in disguise as his own twin brother, faces the unenviable task of arranging a funeral - for himself. Meanwhile, Herr Flick insists his senior officers attend in a bid to seek out members of the Resistance. Comedy, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels 1,500 miles in a journey from Cape Town in South Africa to the Victoria Falls in Zimbabwe, following a hugely ambitious railway pioneered by 19th-century British colonial politician Cecil Rhodes. He starts his trip in luxury on the Blue Train, a five-star hotel on rails that takes him to the mining town of Kimberley, where he explores the diamond mine where Rhodes made his fortune

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion

programme.title: Nazi Hunters

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night of the competition in Manchester, yoga teacher Jeannette plans to treat her guests to an evening of poetry and fish. However, things don't get off to a good start with Dina, who hates fish, despite having cooked seafood herself. Dave is wary of Jeannette after her abrupt behaviour, while Mollie is fascinated by her host's eccentric manner. Meanwhile, Spanish-born Oscar thinks he has the competition sewn up when it comes to the food, but are Jeannette's fare and her tales of Clint Eastwood enough to take her into the lead?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Mollie hosts the third dinner party of the week in and around Manchester, hoping to impress her guests with an indulgent, luxurious menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon and Shaun look for a countryside villa for football-mad couple Tracie and Justin from Southend-on-Sea, who are taking early retirement and want to make a permanent move to Spain. In Alicante, Sophie and Manuel are tasked with finding a holiday home for Tony and Ellen from north Wales. Tony is determined to make the most of his golden years after suffering a serious illness, and with a budget of 180,000 euros they're looking for a detached villa, with a couple of unusual requests thrown in

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon and Steve Garner help Huddersfield couple Carole and Graham, who want to move to Spain to be nearer their daughter. With a healthy budget of 240,000 euros, they're looking for the 'wow' factor. In Alicante, the Rodriguez family's clients are foster carer Jayne and her daughter Lianne from Blackpool. They've been holidaying in Spain for years and with a budget of 120,000 euros are looking for a summer getaway with room for all the family

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Sharon and Steve hope to find the dream property for childhood sweethearts Heather and Graham from Lancashire, who are looking for a rustic property with potential to accommodate guests for a planned cycle tour business. Over in Alicante, Linda and Manuel are looking to find a permanent home for Gill from High Wycombe, who's accompanied by her sister, Linda

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sharon and Steve Garner hope to find a permanent home in Almeria for Welsh couple Andrew and Sharon, while Manuel's doctor has challenged him to find a relaxing hobby

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon and Steve meet Rex and Karen from Boston in the USA, who have decided to move to Spain after falling in love with the lifestyle. Karen used to be an estate agent, so she knows exactly what she wants for her 350,000 euros. In Alicante, Linda and her daughter Charlotte also have their work cut out with clients Barbara and Russell from Tamworth, who are looking for a holiday home near the beach for scuba diving enthusiast Russell, on a budget of 130,000 euros

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faces the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company, but can he get through to this defiant pair?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. Randy's hoarding is out of control, while Alexa, who runs the kitchen, has a lot to learn. To make matters worse, the Old Neighborhood is one of the filthiest eateries the fiery chef has ever seen, and only one of the biggest transformations of the series can save the business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from the first series, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. Among the shows under review are Love Island, Naked Attraction, Killing Eve and First Dates, as well as the films Titanic and American Pie

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Glyn, a 60-year-old disco-dancing entertainer from Essex, hopes to find the perfect partner with whom he can dance off into the sunset. Twenty-eight-year-old Jamie has been on lots of dates, but she keeps meeting cheaters who leave her feeling cold, and needs the show's help to find an honest guy. Presented by Anna Richardson

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Mental health nurse Jim counsels people who are struggling with self-isolation, while paramedics Karina and James respond to a call from a man who claims to be armed and is threatening suicide. Despite losing his mother to Covid-19, paramedic Richard continues working on the frontline while also managing his anxiety about taking the virus home to his wife, whose complex health issues put her at high risk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The award-winning show revisits of the most memorable stories filmed since 2011 at King's College and St George's hospitals in south London - places where life, love and loss unfold every day. This episode from King's shows how A&E staff care for a variety of patients, including a delivery driver who has serious injuries after a car crash, a young man who has a broken jaw after being randomly punched by a stranger, and a patient under arrest

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Matt and Sophie White from Sussex as they build a giant family house of fun for themselves and their children. Going against the norm of designing a serious and practical home, the couple's property features a revolving bookcase door, secret dens, hiding places and a fireman's pole. Matt uses a steel frame system to allow them to change the layout of the property, and other changes include amending the window shapes and sizes, and adding a half floor to create a James Bond room

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gemma Willock hosts the first party in the West Midlands, and her evening of glitz and glamour looks set to be a roaring success until an unwelcome revelation about her corner-cutting approach to the competition threatens her chances of taking the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mortgage adviser Andrew Devey throws the second party in the West Midlands, hosting a beatnik-themed evening. He cooks shepherd's pie for his main course, as well as lining up some pop art in the hope that it will expand his guests' minds

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jules Evans hosts the third day of the competition in the West Midlands, and plans to indulge her passion for historical re-enactments by planning a medieval-themed dinner. The guests offer mixed responses when they look at the menu, but things take a turn for the better when she announces a duel will form part of the evening's entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Massage therapist Lucy Hird hosts the fourth dinner party from the West Midlands, featuring an extensive menu full of Thai specialities. She also gives individual presents to her guests, but her night is compromised by the entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mental-health nurse Lloyd Cast hosts the final dinner party from the West Midlands. Despite appearing to be an extrovert so far, he decides on a chilled theme for the evening to allow his food to take centre stage, before the winner of the £1,000 is announced

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: A trio of couples compete to be the best dinner party hosts in Coventry, beginning with joker Neal and his level-headed wife Hazel, who let themselves down with a few cooking errors. Ed and Jess keep things simple on day two, although a secret from Neal's past has the diners in hysterics, before Emma and Huseyin round the contest off with a Turkish menu

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Berkshire compete to host the best dinner party and walk away with the cash prize of £1,000, with Julian and Lesley, who prepare a meat-heavy menu, Tash and Rob, whose healthy meal has an unwelcome ingredient in the dessert, and Chris and Amanda, who recite their wedding vows during the evening

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows five apartments to Nottingham-based social worker Sheila, who wants to buy a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin lines up five properties for Michael and his wife Jane from Sussex, who are looking for a holiday home within easy reach of the coast in Spain's Almeria province. They have a budget of £90,000 and dream of finding a traditional property with modern features that the whole family can enjoy

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton is on the Portuguese island of Madeira, helping house-hunters Sandy and Phil find the perfect holiday home to enjoy with their children and grandchildren

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps John and Soussan search for a holiday home on Spain's Costa Calida. The pair hope to find an apartment with a pool for when their grandchildren come to visit

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis in Woodland Park, Colorado, where guests have a choice between casual fine-dining service or a pick-up from the drive-through takeaway. Hard-nosed owner Julie finds herself completely at odds with the head chef, forcing her to breaking point

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the conclusion of Gordon Ramsay's visit to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, he confronts sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum, and finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the Italian restaurant's failings

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Brooklyn, New York, to tackle a 55-year-old family business. Owner John Esposito left school at the age of 14 and has worked at his parents' restaurant, Sal's Pizzeria, ever since. Their success meant they could afford to purchase the location next door in the 1990s, naming it Mama Maria after John's mother, who died in 2002. But when he lost his father as well, John became overwhelmed by the responsibility of running both establishments and his livelihood is at risk

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Wilkinsburg in Pennsylvania, home to Ms Jean's Southern Cuisine, which was opened by retired special-education teacher Jean Gould. During the chef's attempt to save the business from disaster, he discovers her soul-food restaurant resembles a dentist's waiting room and finds a lot of tension between Jean and her employees, who claim she is a Jekyll-and-Hyde character

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bob's in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. Every year profits nosedive through the bleak winter months, and matters aren't improved when it becomes clear they have both lost their passion - not only for the business but also for each other. With the restaurant and marriage on the line, Gordon tries to help turn things around before it's too late

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the most difficult cases he has taken on since the series began. First, he returns to Cafe Hon in Baltimore, Maryland, where owner Denise Whiting was trapped in a nasty battle with the community and had threatened the locals with lawsuits. Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. On his final stop, Gordon travels to Leone's in Montclair, New Jersey, where Rose Leone was depending on her lazy son Michael to run the restaurant

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Matt and Sophie White from Sussex as they build a giant family house of fun for themselves and their children. Going against the norm of designing a serious and practical home, the couple's property features a revolving bookcase door, secret dens, hiding places and a fireman's pole. Matt uses a steel frame system to allow them to change the layout of the property, and other changes include amending the window shapes and sizes, and adding a half floor to create a James Bond room

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from the first series, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. Among the shows under review are Love Island, Naked Attraction, Killing Eve and First Dates, as well as the films Titanic and American Pie

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of house-hunters looking for homes in the historic city of York. Kirstie helps first-time buyers Tom and Timna, who met at university, married soon after and are now expecting their first child. With the lease on their rental almost up, time is of the essence find them their family home. Phil is searching for Yorkshire-born Rich and Kirsty, who have been living it up in London but want to move back up North to be closer to their family - and to start their own

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Mental health nurse Jim counsels people who are struggling with self-isolation, while paramedics Karina and James respond to a call from a man who claims to be armed and is threatening suicide. Despite losing his mother to Covid-19, paramedic Richard continues working on the frontline while also managing his anxiety about taking the virus home to his wife, whose complex health issues put her at high risk

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The semi-finals have arrived, with four teams battling for a place in the final, with the challenges including five hours to create a magical Charlotte Russe display, while at the same time preparing all of the elements needed to perform a spectacular, World of Magic-inspired piece of gourmet theatre, known as live plating. At the end of the challenge, the teams have just three minutes to transform their table into a gastronomic work of illusion and theatre, under the tough gaze of judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the White Lodge Hotel in Filey, North Yorkshire, where hosts James and Kim Hodgson hope their love of high standards will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Amanda Lucia-Charley and her friend Alan Talbot invite the other hoteliers to sample the delights of Crystals on the Prom in Blackpool, where Alan introduces his alter-ego Ada

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Old Red Lion Country Inn in Upper Poppleton, North Yorkshire, where pair Mark and Jean Lee hope their plain and simple pub B&B will impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest ends at glamping site Camp Katur in Kirklington, North Yorkshire, where Kerry Roy and Nicola Wilding hope to wow their guests with their unique 'geodomes' and see-through pods

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the contest arrives, and while Amanda Lucia-Charley and Alan Talbot receive harsh criticism of their breakfast, Mark and Jean Lee's decor receives scathing reviews

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-lover Kerry Bolwell-Williams kicks off a week of dinner parties in the Welsh valleys, where she hopes that a full choir and a simple menu will impress her guests and make her an early contender for the £1,000 prize. However, she does not react well when it is suggested that her home has a rather distinctive smell

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Food-lover and theme park fan Dave Morris hosts the second dinner party in the Welsh valleys, hoping his home-grown herbs, hand-picked fruit and complicated menu will win him the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shop owner Tasnim Sardar hosts the third night of the competition in the Welsh valleys, and hopes her Pakistani-inspired party will secure her the £1,000 prize. Financial advisor Gill Leonard likes her curries to be hot and spicy, and is particularly pleased by Tasnim's curried cauliflower

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ryan Woodward hosts on the fourth night in the Welsh valleys and has put a lot of thought into his evening, but each of his courses goes wrong - from a bread blunder to a clam crisis. He hopes an Ibizan dance lesson and drinks served in his hot tub will help to turn the party around

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in the Welsh valleys, financial advisor Gill Leonard dons the oven gloves to entertain the other contestants, and there is a dramatic start to the evening thanks to a mock fire-breathing dragon. Later, the host makes a remarkable revelation to her shocked guests

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton is on the Portuguese island of Madeira, helping house-hunters Sandy and Phil find the perfect holiday home to enjoy with their children and grandchildren

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Denise and Roger have fallen in love with the Breton culture and lifestyle. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties in north-west France for their £120,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Just B in Edinburgh, where owners Karen MacDonald and Geoff Thomson hope their slice of zen will win over their guests

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon and Shaun look for a countryside villa for football-mad couple Tracie and Justin from Southend-on-Sea, who are taking early retirement and want to make a permanent move to Spain. In Alicante, Sophie and Manuel are tasked with finding a holiday home for Tony and Ellen from north Wales. Tony is determined to make the most of his golden years after suffering a serious illness, and with a budget of 180,000 euros they're looking for a detached villa, with a couple of unusual requests thrown in

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The apartment block board's president denies Frasier's request to attach a knocker to his front door, leaving the shrink determined to fight his neighbour's tyranny. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Eddie appears depressed, so Martin enlists the aid of a dog psychiatrist to determine the root of his misery, much to Frasier and Niles's amusement. However, the diagnosis is that Eddie is picking up negative vibes from everyone around him, causing the Cranes and Daphne to assess their own lives. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows five apartments to Nottingham-based social worker Sheila, who wants to buy a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Steve and Sandra met while serving in the Royal Navy, and now that Steve is on the verge of retirement they're looking for a Spanish bolthole to 'wind down' in. They love the area around Casares, so Jasmine Harman is on hand to show them five available properties within their £100,000 budget

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Brooklyn, New York, to tackle a 55-year-old family business. Owner John Esposito left school at the age of 14 and has worked at his parents' restaurant, Sal's Pizzeria, ever since. Their success meant they could afford to purchase the location next door in the 1990s, naming it Mama Maria after John's mother, who died in 2002. But when he lost his father as well, John became overwhelmed by the responsibility of running both establishments and his livelihood is at risk

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

With the lease on their rental almost up, time is of the essence find them their family home. Phil is searching for Yorkshire-born Rich and Kirsty, who have been living it up in London but want to move back up North to be closer to their family - and to start their own

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets an old friend in Norfolk who is planning to turn an old sewage works into a unique chill-out space for his family, complete with a self-supporting dome roof and a circular carp pond. A Midlands father and daughter work together on salvaging a dilapidated childhood treehouse with a budget of just of £300, George and Will find unusual inspiration as they continue building their own treehouse, and in Japan, George visits a breath-taking chapel made of intertwining concrete ribbons

programme.title: The Real EastEnders
programme.desc: Filmed in the summer of 2019 by first-time director Ashley Francis-Roy, this documentary presents the world as seen through the eyes of children growing up on council estates on the Isle of Dogs. Brendan is a sixth-generation Islander who's lived with his nan Lynne since he was placed in care when he was four, while 12-year-old Leslie wants to be an electrician, as well as a part-time rapper and gamer - but his mum Tina worries about him hanging out with boys who seek respect and easy money

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: As the UK enters its fourth week of lockdown, West Midlands Ambulance Service comes to the aid of the staff of care homes, who are feeling forgotten as the virus claims the lives of so many residents. It falls on crews like Julie and Adam to provide help and reassurance. Back in the control centre, Irum has had fast-track training and is plunged into the stark realities of her new role, while a national minute's silence for NHS and care workers holds a particular significance for her

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary charting the step-by-step process of the construction of an electric London taxi, from the pieces of aluminium chassis to the £60,000 power train eco-cab

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: In the Devon port of Brixham, trawlerman Dave Driver sets out to sea on his boat in search of cuttlefish, while Mark and Isobelle Delbridge are determined to make a success of their new dairy farm on the southern edge of Bodmin Moor. On the northern reaches of Dartmoor National Park, horseback farmers Crispin Alford, his wife Diana and son Steve are preparing to sell their hill ponies

programme.title: Jimmy's Forest
programme.desc: A thick layer of snow covers the Norfolk wood as winter arrives, helping Jimmy Doherty trace the prints of birds and animals using it as their home. He also learns about how insects survive the season's harsh conditions, watches 80,000 rooks take to the sky, builds a Yukon firepit and discovers evidence that an unwelcome predator is residing in the forest

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Hull resident Peter and his mum Greta employ the skills of Jonnie Irwin to help find him his dream property in Huelva in Spain on a £150,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Oaklea Barns Guest House in Southport, Merseyside, where Mandi Magee and Paul Green think their impeccable standards will impress

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan feels inadequate when he realises he cannot shower Lindsey with gifts the way Charlie does with Courtney. Desperate to gain ground, he offers massages at the mall for extra money, but when this venture fails to bring in a decent profit, he decides to resort to desperate measures

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan forbids Lindsey from inviting Charlie to her housewarming party, convinced his womanising brother is about to make a move on her. However, the plan backfires and he ends up getting banned himself - forcing him to spy on proceedings from the outside

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan receives investment money from family members for his booming chiropractic business, and pockets their contribution instead of using it to further his plans. Meanwhile, Gordon delivers a pizza to Rose's house, and catches sight of her new husband

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is tricked into fearing for his safety as his so-called affair continues, and Rose finds out how all Alan's investments are being spent. Desperate to keep his scam under wraps, he follows her home to beg for her silence, but ends up discovering the truth about her husband Manny. The last ever episode featuring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie Harper is shocked when his brother leaves his wife and asks to move in with him - with his son in tow. Initially reluctant to take in the new arrivals, he soon warms to having them around when the boy displays an unexpected skill for poker and proves useful in attracting women

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake entices a flock of seagulls into his room, while Charlie tries to persuade him to obey a few rules. Alan finds himself continually summoned to Judith's house to do odd jobs. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan resolves to enjoy a weekend at Disneyland with his son - but when it goes spectacularly wrong, he settles for the company of Charlie and vast quantities of alcohol. The pair eventually decide to track down their mother and tell her what they think of her. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie's cleaning lady walks out in exasperation over the increasing demands being placed on her, so Alan offers to take over as housekeeper - but proves unsuited to the role. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Jon Cryer

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie attends one of Jake's soccer games and ends up hitting it off with another player's mother. Meanwhile, Alan's attempt to help with the team's organisation leads to two women fighting over him. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie teaches Jake to ignore unwanted female telephone callers, which backfires when Evelyn turns up unannounced. She drags him off to dinner with her new boyfriend and his daughter - who turns out to be one of the women Charlie has studiously avoided ringing. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie and Mia begin to make wedding arrangements, but things fall apart when their families meet, leading them to consider running off and marrying in Las Vegas. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.desc: Charlie returns to his bachelor lifestyle, while Alan is depressed following his divorce from Kandi. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie finally tells Alan why he and Mia broke up - she had wanted the playboy to evict his brother and nephew from the house. The pair also deal with a noisy neighbour

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Enforcement officers are faced with the difficult job of evicting a woman with two children - a task that takes a turn for the worse when her ex-partner makes an appearance. In Liverpool, locals threaten a riot over an unpaid debt at a computer shop, while two agents come across an extremely rare car in the course of their duties

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. Opening it up, they realise they urgently need police back-up - and the property quickly becomes a crime scene. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town FC to collect a debt, while Paul's team arrives at a property in Essex to find that they have to remove a woman and her six children

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul, Steve and Ben make a shocking discovery when they break into a home in Essex while serving an eviction notice on a single mother and her two children. Meanwhile, Stewart and Iain receive more than just threats from the staff and owner of a taxi business, a car dealer has to choose between paying up or losing his showroom stars, and an Indian restaurant owner is in danger of losing his livelihood

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve make an extraordinary discovery inside a locked bedroom, and immediately call for police back-up. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town football club to recover a debt, while Paul and Ben have difficulty evicting a couple from a house in Kent after learning they have six children and nowhere else to go

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: After moving abroad for work, a landlord discovers that his tenants have left him over £20,000 behind with his mortgage, and the developers of an old swimming pool in Bristol discover that they are liable for the electricity that the building's squatters have been using

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Ian Huntley: 5 Mistakes That Caught the Killer

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Columbo: Dagger of the Mind (1972)
programme.desc: A London theatre producer discovers his actress wife is having an affair with her co-star in their latest production. To avoid exposure, the pair murder him and make it look as if his death was an accident. Meanwhile, Columbo is in the city to pick up some tips from Scotland Yard, and sets about uncovering the truth. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Honor Blackman, Richard Basehart and Wilfrid Hyde White

programme.title: Columbo: Short Fuse (1972)
programme.desc: The son of a chemical company's founder is blackmailed by his uncle, who wants to force him into selling the business. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Roddy McDowall and Ida Lupino

But she reckons without the intervention of Columbo, who begins laying his trap for her while also nursing a neck injury. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Trish Van Devere

programme.title: Columbo: By Dawn's Early Light (1974)

However, Columbo is not fooled by the deception and sets out to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Patrick McGoohan, Tom Simcox and Mark Wheeler

programme.title: Columbo: Candidate for Crime (1973)

Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Jackie Cooper, Ken Swofford and Tisha Sterling

Meanwhile, Director Vance and FBI Agent Fornell travel to London as part of the ongoing case involving the escaped British spies

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team, alongside the FBI and MI6, continues the international manhunt for a disgraced British spy who is seeking revenge on the agents he blames for his wife's death. A re-examination of the fugitive's conviction for selling military intelligence reveals that he may have been framed - and the identity of the only person who can clear his name

Concerns are raised about whether the unit has been irreparably compromised, and FBI special agent Tammy Gregorio is assigned to discover the truth. However, the agents are quickly given a chance to prove their effectiveness, as they co-ordinate with various other federal agencies to track down a sniper targeting special events across New Orleans. Starring Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans

Determined to keep the matter within the Navy, Pride and his colleagues manage to get hold of crucial evidence, forcing the FBI to work with them

programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: Simon and Conrad are embroiled in a high-speed pursuit with a driver who seemingly has a death wish, and officers encounter an adult-film star who moonlights as a drug dealer

programme.title: Border Patrol
programme.desc: Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders.

programme.title: Border Patrol
programme.desc: Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders.

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports as customs and immigration officers tackle drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Another edition of the fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports, following customs and immigration officers as they go about their daily business of tackling drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists

programme.title: Border Security USA
programme.desc: A routine drug search in New York turns into a race to save a man's life, and officers in Miami are forced to evacuate an airport terminal

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of neighbourhood policing teams. Officers investigate a suspicious car accident in a remote wildlife park by the Yorkshire coast - and discover that the vehicle was used by a burglar hoping to make a speedy getaway from the scene of the crime. Plus, neighbourhood police from the Humberside force use social media to make the public aware of an arrest, and a team in Bridlington, East Yorkshire, keeps worried onlookers at bay as members of the emergency services tackle a three-storey fire in which two residents are trapped. Presented by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: A shy officer with an irrational fear of the transporter beam finally overcomes his phobia - only to encounter sinister forces while en route to a trapped spaceship. Sci-fi drama, guest starring Dwight Schultz

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: The team investigate the murder of a window washer and find that the victim is actually a renowned street artist, while Grover and Adam look into the murder of a gambler

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Morgana makes a sinister pact with Nemeth and King Odin, using Princess Mithian as bait to lure Arthur to his death - a deception so powerful that the whole of Camelot, even Merlin, is taken in. Can the young warlock see through the lies before it is too late? Fantasy adventure, starring Colin Morgan, Bradley James, Katie McGrath and Janet Montgomery

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A detective whose career was just starting out in the 1980s discusses investigating Peter Sutcliffe, the notorious Yorkshire Ripper, who murdered 13 women between 1975 and 1981

programme.title: Killer Colleagues
programme.desc: The story of three professors who were shot dead on a university campus by their resentful colleague, after she was denied tenure

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: A man is killed in a wilderness fire, and Grissom's inquiries lead him to a second victim - a badly burned woman who is miraculously still alive and may have started the blaze. Meanwhile, Catherine, Nick and Warrick try to solve a case involving the death of an entire family. As inquiries proceed, a link is found between the two investigations

programme.title: Brit Cops
programme.desc: Documentary series in which cameras follow the work of police officers in Lincolnshire as they tackle drug-related and violent crime. Narrated by Daniel Abineri

programme.title: Road Wars

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of fun-loving hippie Michael Morey, who agreed to smuggle 4kg of cocaine from Ecuador to Madrid

programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of police forces across Australia as they deal with situations from sieges and major drug busts, to murder investigations
