programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band is kidnapped by an old enemy, who experiments on the girls to give them super intelligence - but make them so boring that no one will listen to them

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls investigate a plan to rig a male beauty contest by transforming the finalists

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby challenges Vic to prove his courage by spending the night alone in a forest full of hungry wolves

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar is challenged to a race by a haughty rival captain with a spectacular boat - but Vic hatches a plan to give the Drakkar an extra burst of speed

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra suggests arranging a Present Day on the island, but struggles when he is asked to find a gift for Vicky

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra realises that the island needs tidying up and organises a scavenger hunt to make everyone participate

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar receives a message in a bottle containing a plea for help from a damsel in distress, and Vic realises the ship is infested with woodworm

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The crew is kidnapped by a tribe of British warrior women, who force Halvar to prove he is innocent of plundering their kingdom by undertaking four challenges

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra wants Zara and Lawrence to take a nap so they can watch the shooting stars later that night

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra discovers a variety of flowers on the island and decides to collect them all

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb presents the best properties she found on her real-estate tour of Florida and the Caribbean in the show that aims to advise British househunters looking for a second home abroad

programme.title: Go Kart Go (1964)
programme.desc: A group of children builds a go kart for a race, but a rival team plots to sabotage their efforts. Family drama, starring Dennis Waterman and Frazer Hines

programme.title: A Stitch in Time (1963)
programme.desc: A butcher's bumbling assistant causes no end of accidents at work, the latest of which has landed his boss in hospital. While visiting, he meets a little girl who has not spoken since her parents died, and resolves to put a smile on the orphan's face. Slapstick comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Jerry Desmonde, Lucy Appleby and Jill Melford

programme.title: The Big Money (1956)
programme.desc: An incompetent thief finally manages to make his family of crooks proud of him by pulling off a major robbery, but finds his ill-gotten gains bring him anything but happiness. Comedy, starring Ian Carmichael, Belinda Lee, Kathleen Harrison and Robert Helpmann

programme.title: The Fast Lady (1963)
programme.desc: A cycling enthusiast on a modest civil servant's salary gives up his beloved bike to a buy a luxury car to impress a local magistrate's daughter. However, he first has to pass his driving test to stand any chance of winning her heart - and in the process ends up in a wild car-chase after a gang of robbers. Comedy, with Julie Christie, Stanley Baker, James Robertson Justice, Leslie Phillips and Dick Emery

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises a couple looking for a holiday home in Cyprus for under £70,000, weighing up the pros and cons of a mountain lodge, a 200-year-old village house and a luxury villa

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Supermarket Foods
programme.desc: Cherry Healey presents the second of two programmes in which she explores the unexpected positive health benefits of everyday foods. Here she learns about the brain-boosting capabilities of the humble berry, testing it out on a group of students to see whether it aids their concentration, and reveals the daily staples that can help with everything from the heart to the teeth

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era

programme.title: Henry VIII's Enforcer: The Rise and Fall of Thomas Cromwell
programme.desc: Lawyer and statesman Thomas Cromwell served as chief minister to Henry VIII from 1532 to 1540 and has gone down in history as one of the most corrupt and manipulative people ever to hold power in England. In this documentary Diarmaid MacCulloch reveals another side to the man, arguing that Cromwell was a principled idealist and revolutionary whose radical evangelism laid the foundations for the modern British state

programme.title: Fallen Soldier
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the tragic events surrounding the final fight of boxer Gerald McClellan

programme.title: The Chant of Jimmie Blacksmith (1978)
programme.desc: An Australian farmhand of half-Aboriginal descent tries to get by in the face of cruelty and prejudice. A betrayal by his white employers tips him over the edge, and he embarks on a campaign of violent revenge for all the injustices he has endured. Historical drama, starring Tommy Lewis and Freddy Reynolds

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham

programme.title: Queen of the Blues (1979)
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a failing Soho nightclub and hope to turn its fortunes around - but they end up being threatened by gangsters demanding protection money. Fortunately, salvation is at hand in an enthusiastic stripper, who comes up with a plan to save the day. Drama, starring Mary Millington and Rosemary England

programme.title: Silent Scream (1990)
programme.desc: A former soldier is sentenced to life in prison after murdering a bartender in 1960s Soho. During his incarceration, his violent behaviour gets more and more out of control, until prison doctors assign him an experimental treatment to help him come to terms with the roots of his rage. Prison drama, starring Iain Glen and Robert Carlyle

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: John Quilter cooks 'posh' mushrooms on toast, taxi driver Joey Bone tries out three Italian restaurants, and one of London's few female butchers is interviewed

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Camden and Sydenham for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Henry VIII's Enforcer: The Rise and Fall of Thomas Cromwell
programme.desc: Lawyer and statesman Thomas Cromwell served as chief minister to Henry VIII from 1532 to 1540 and has gone down in history as one of the most corrupt and manipulative people ever to hold power in England. In this documentary Diarmaid MacCulloch reveals another side to the man, arguing that Cromwell was a principled idealist and revolutionary whose radical evangelism laid the foundations for the modern British state

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Supermarket Foods
programme.desc: Cherry Healey presents the second of two programmes in which she explores the unexpected positive health benefits of everyday foods. Here she learns about the brain-boosting capabilities of the humble berry, testing it out on a group of students to see whether it aids their concentration, and reveals the daily staples that can help with everything from the heart to the teeth

programme.title: Operation Third Form (1966)
programme.desc: A trio of youngsters tries to stop a gang of crooks and save an old junk collector from being accused of theft. Children's drama, starring Kevin Bennett and Sydney Bromley

programme.title: Hide and Seek (1972)
programme.desc: A boy runs away from a young offenders' institute to find his father, whom he discovers is planning a bank robbery. Crime drama, starring Peter Newby and Gary Kemp

programme.title: The Big Money (1956)
programme.desc: An incompetent thief finally manages to make his family of crooks proud of him by pulling off a major robbery, but finds his ill-gotten gains bring him anything but happiness. Comedy, starring Ian Carmichael, Belinda Lee, Kathleen Harrison and Robert Helpmann

programme.title: The Rats of Tobruk (1944)
programme.desc: Two tough Australian soldiers fight alongside an intellectual Englishman in the North African campaign. They become involved in a critical siege, and their fearless efforts manage to hold up the German advance for months. Second World War drama, starring Grant Taylor, Peter Finch and Chips Rafferty

programme.title: Victim (1961)
programme.desc: A secretly gay barrister discovers his lover has been driven to suicide by blackmailers, who have also extorted money from several other men. Risking his reputation and marriage, he hunts down the culprits, fully aware that it could lead to his own exposure as a homosexual, illegal at the time. Drama, starring Dirk Bogarde, Sylvia Syms, Dennis Price and Anthony Nicholls

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises a couple looking for a holiday home in Cyprus for under £70,000, weighing up the pros and cons of a mountain lodge, a 200-year-old village house and a luxury villa

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb takes a mother and daughter on a tour of tempting properties along Turkey's Turquoise Coast, including a huge mountain house with garden and sea views - a bargain at £42,000

programme.title: Capital Conversation

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike is having financial trouble, so Laura decides to help out. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Abraham's Point (2008)
programme.desc: A man who left his childhood home in Wales 15 years previously decides to face up to his troubled past. He steals a grandfather clock and takes it on a journey across the country, intending to return the vintage timepiece to his father, who is dying. Drama, starring Mackenzie Crook and Joseph Milson

programme.title: A Random Encounter (2013)
programme.desc: Two strangers trying to make their way in Hollywood meet by chance in a coffee shop, and come away unable to stop thinking about one another. However, they both soon discover that they have no way of tracking the other down. Romantic comedy, starring Abby Wathen, Michael Rady, Meghan Markle and Sean Young

programme.title: Maeve (1981)
programme.desc: A woman returns to her roots in Belfast after several years away. Her return leaves many of the people she left behind wrestling with unresolved issues from the past, while she is also forced to confront old memories. Drama, starring Mary Jackson and Mark Mulholland

programme.title: Fallen Soldier
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the tragic events surrounding the final fight of boxer Gerald McClellan

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar and his crew throws all the food overboard to appease a monster they think is chasing them - but then have to go ashore in dangerous territory to find more

programme.title: Vic the Viking

programme.title: Tashi

programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Norwich couple Simon and Caitlin embark on an ambitious project under the watchful eye of expert Sarah Beeny. They plan to buy a former council house, invest all their savings and completely renovate it in the hope that a subsequent sale will raise enough money to move onto the next rung of the property ladder

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part

programme.title: Calculated Risk (1963)
programme.desc: An ex-convict plans one last daring heist, by tunneling into a bank vault through the basement of a neighbouring house. Crime drama, starring William Lucas and John Rutland

programme.title: The Case of the Frightened Lady (1940)
programme.desc: A woman employed as a secretary to her aristocratic aunt has to contend with sinister servants plotting behind her back and a scheme by her employer to marry her off to her own cousin. The situation is complicated further when the family's doctor is accused of murder. Thriller, starring Marius Goring and Penelope Dudley-Ward

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Camden and Sydenham for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises British house-hunters looking for a place in Crete, showing them round a traditional Greek home, a modern village property and an old Venetian-style farm, each for less than £60,000

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A south London couple gamble their savings in an attempt to raise enough cash to buy their dream home in the countryside. The plan is to renovate their two-bedroom former council flat so they can make money by renting it out for as much as possible. Property developer Sarah Beeny keeps an eye on their progress

programme.title: I Was There: The Great War Interviews
programme.desc: In the early 1960s, the BBC spoke to almost 300 people about their experiences during the First World War for landmark series The Great War, but only a fraction of the interviews were broadcast. This programme airs a selection of the original material, most of which has never been screened before, with recollections of the horrors of artillery bombardment and fleeting outbreaks of peace on the battlefield, as well as the stories of those on the home front waiting for news of their loved ones. Narrated by Samuel West

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Hitler's deputy, Rudolf Hess, the third in line to leadership of Nazi Germany, who was declared insane by the Fuehrer after making an unexpected errand of peace to Britain in 1941. The senior Nazi's fitness to stand trial was at the centre of his case in the tribunal. Narrated by Matthew MacFadyen and starring Ben Cross as Hess

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?

programme.title: UCMMA
programme.desc: Some of the UK's finest Mixed Martial Arts fighters tear it up in the cage with intense action from East London's iconic venue, The Troxy

programme.title: Warrior Challenge
programme.desc: MMA & K1 from the Troxy in London title fight Mihai Zmarandescu vs Alfie Hawes plus Erol Raif vs George Zorilov, Kat Freeman vs Kat Twardowska

programme.title: Bare Knuckle
programme.desc: Documentary exploring bare knuckle boxing's rise in popularity, its evolution through centuries, legal issues & the fight to give modern day boxers professional recognition

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)
programme.desc: A gang of lads selling drugs in Darndale in Dublin's Northside sets out to gain more money and power and enter the big leagues of the narcotics trade. Things begin to unravel quickly as not everyone in the area is willing to let them achieve the notoriety they crave without a fight. Thriller, starring John Connors and Fionn Walton

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Cabbie Joey Bone road-tests three burger bars, Kerryann Dunlop whips up a mean chicken stir fry, and Tottenham's soul food sisters do their trademark curry goat

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts a festive edition of the game show. Loose Women's Sherrie Hewson and Lisa Maxwell are joined by their former co-panellist Denise Welch to take on the Cube together, aiming to complete a series of fiendishly difficult trials to raise money for charity. Olympic long-jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford also tackles the challenge, but will he match fellow athlete Mo Farah's record and scoop the jackpot?
